sagesses.texte: Seekers travelled far to find a master so perfect, it was said, that he cast no shadow. When they reached him, the sun drew his shadow just as it does for any man. Disappointed, they were on the point of leaving. The master smiled: « You came seeking one who has no shadow; seek rather to become, yourselves, transparent. »
sagesses.commentaire: We chase after perfect beings so as to be spared from becoming such ourselves. But no one walks the road in our place. The shadow you carry is not a flaw to flee: it is the part of you still opaque to the light. Today, instead of admiring another's purity, ask yourself what small veil, within you, you might let fall.

sagesses.texte: Two painters competed. One covered his wall with sumptuous frescoes. The other did nothing but polish the wall facing it until it became a mirror. When the curtain was drawn back, the polished wall returned the whole fresco, more alive still.
sagesses.commentaire: The heart need not accumulate in order to shine: it suffices for it to be cleansed. All the beauty of the world is already reflected in a soul made limpid. Rather than adding knowledge, possessions, images, ask yourself what you might erase today. With the rust gone, the real appears of its own accord.

sagesses.texte: The drop was asked: « What do you fear? » It answered: « To vanish into the sea. » The sage said: « It is by losing yourself there that you will become the sea. »
sagesses.commentaire: We cling to our little casing, believing it protects us, when in truth it confines us. What we call the loss of self is often an enlargement. Wherever you hold on most tightly today — a pride, a fear, a role — there lies, perhaps, the invitation to surrender and become more vast.

sagesses.texte: Musk has no need to say that it is musk: its fragrance speaks for it.
sagesses.commentaire: What is true within us has no need to proclaim itself. The goodness that announces itself evaporates; the goodness that keeps silent gives off perfume. Today, do good without flagging it, pray without showing it, love without claiming it. What you hide from the eyes of men, the Real breathes in.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin was found on all fours, searching beneath a street lamp. « I have lost my key. » They searched with him, in vain. « Where exactly did you lose it? » « Over there, in my house. » « Then why search here? » « Because here there is light. »
sagesses.commentaire: We seek happiness where it is easy, not where it lies. In noise, in distraction, in the outer world — because it is lit, accessible. But the key is in the inner house, in the darkness that frightens us. Today, dare to search where you avoid looking.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin had lost his donkey and was thanking God aloud. « You give thanks for a lost donkey? » « Yes: had I been upon it, I should be lost as well. »
sagesses.commentaire: Every loss conceals a grace we cannot yet see. What seems to us a misfortune often spares us a greater one. Today, before what vexes you, look for the angle from which you might say thank you. Gratitude does not deny the pain: it opens it onto something vaster than itself.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin was reproached for riding his donkey backwards, facing the tail. « It is not I who am back to front, » he replied. « It is the donkey who chose to go the other way. »
sagesses.commentaire: How often do we judge as absurd the one who sees differently, without asking which of us two is looking the right way? The sage holds the reins even when his mount contradicts him. Today, before correcting another, first check the direction of your own gaze.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin scattered salt over his field to make it grow, as one salts a soup. Nothing came up. « Strange, » he said: « what gives savour to the one kills the other. »
sagesses.commentaire: No single recipe holds for everything. What nourishes one heart renders another barren; what saved you yesterday may shut you in today. Beware of ready-made formulas, even spiritual ones. Today, listen to what the ground before you truly asks, rather than repeating your habitual gesture.

sagesses.titre: The Blind Men and the Elephant
sagesses.texte: In the dark, men touched an elephant. One grasped the trunk: « a pipe. » Another the ear: « a fan. » Another the leg: « a pillar. » They quarrelled all night long, each certain he held the whole truth.
sagesses.commentaire: Each one touches a fragment of the Real and believes he holds it entire. Our certainties are pieces taken for the whole. Peace begins when we admit that the other, too, touches something true. Today, in a disagreement, ask yourself: « Which part of the elephant is he speaking of? »

sagesses.texte: It was asked why the nightingale sings all night beside the rose. « Because it loves, » said the sage. « And one who loves does not sleep. »
sagesses.commentaire: True love is a vigil, not a slumber. It asks nothing of the rose, does not pluck it: it simply sings at being near it. Today, love something or someone wanting nothing in return, only for the joy of presence. It is thus that the soul learns to love the Real.

sagesses.titre: What the Thief Could Not Take
sagesses.texte: A thief broke into a hermit's dwelling and found nothing to steal. As he left, ashamed, he heard: « Here, take at least this moonlight at the window. » The hermit possessed nothing — and therefore everything.
sagesses.commentaire: Whoever possesses nothing can lose nothing, and becomes rich in all things. Our goods hold us as much as we hold them. The moon, the sky, the breath: no one can steal these from us. Today, savour one of those treasures no one can take from you, and loosen your grip on the rest.

sagesses.texte: The cage door was opened, but the bird remained. « Why do you not fly away? » It answered: « I have forgotten that I had wings. »
sagesses.commentaire: The heaviest chains are those we no longer see. Habit makes us mistake the cage for the whole world. Often the door is already open; all that is wanting is to remember that we are free. Today, name one habit that holds you back — and try out your wings.

sagesses.texte: A man dreamed of a treasure hidden in a distant city. He journeyed there at length. Once there, a stranger told him: « I too dreamed of a treasure, buried beneath the hearth of a house like yours. » The man returned home and dug beneath his own hearth: the treasure was there.
sagesses.commentaire: What we seek at the ends of the earth often sleeps beneath our feet. The journey was not useless: one had to set out in order to learn to come back and see. Today, look at your ordinary life — your home, your work, those close to you — as the very place where the treasure lies buried.

sagesses.texte: A mother brought her son to the sage so that he might forbid the boy sugar. « Come back in a week. » A week later, the sage simply told the child: « Stop eating sugar. » The mother wondered at the delay. « A week ago, I was still eating it myself. »
sagesses.commentaire: We can only pass on what we have first lived. Counsel that does not come from our own flesh rings hollow. Before asking another to change, the sage begins with himself. Today, what you expect of someone close to you, ask whether you already embody it.

sagesses.texte: A fish asked another: « Where is this sea everyone speaks of? » The other answered: « It is that in which you have been swimming since your birth. »
sagesses.commentaire: What is nearest is the most invisible. We seek the sacred far off when it bathes us at every instant: in the breath, the light, the beating of the heart. Today, pause for a moment and simply feel that you are already borne up by what you seek.

sagesses.texte: One candle lit a hundred others, and its own flame did not diminish.
sagesses.commentaire: The good we give does not run dry: it multiplies. The fear of lack makes us hoard; but light, love, knowledge grow by being shared. Today, light another flame — a word, a gesture, an attention — without fearing you will have less. You will have more.

sagesses.titre: It Is the Coat They Speak To
sagesses.texte: Poorly dressed, Nasreddin was ignored at a banquet. He went home, put on his finest coat, returned, and was welcomed with honour. So he dipped his sleeve into the dish: « Eat, my coat! It is you they invited. »
sagesses.commentaire: The world bows before the garment and forgets the man beneath. We chase after appearances, believing them to be us. Today, ask yourself what your worth is when stripped of its coat — title, status, image. What remains then is you.

sagesses.texte: A royal falcon, raised among hens, pecked and clucked as they did. It was carried up to a height and told: « You are a falcon, the sky is yours. » Then it spread wings it never knew it had, and soared away.
sagesses.commentaire: The soul forgets its nobility through living bent low. Accustomed to the ground, we take our limits for our nature. Yet something within us is made for the sky. Today, remember that you are not merely your habits and your fears: there is a part of you that knows how to fly.

sagesses.texte: Two seeds lay in the earth. One said: « I want to grow, to break through towards the light, » and it sprouted. The other was afraid: « If I come out, I shall be trodden on, plucked; better to stay sheltered. » A hen scratched it up and ate it.
sagesses.commentaire: Refusing to grow out of fear does not protect us: it exposes us in another way. Life asks that we take the risk of opening. In wanting too much to preserve ourselves, we lose ourselves without ever having lived. Today, where fear tells you « stay hidden, » ask whether it is not protecting you... from life itself.

sagesses.texte: A scholar came to question a master. The master poured tea into his cup, and went on pouring once it was full. « It is overflowing! » « Like you: you are so full of what you know that nothing more can enter. »
sagesses.commentaire: One does not learn with a full cup. Our certainties stop up the ear of the spirit. To receive, one must first empty oneself, become poor and open again. Today, approach a person or an idea as if you knew nothing: you will be surprised by what can then fill you.

sagesses.texte: A man galloped along, frantic. « Where are you racing to? » « I am seeking the fragrance with which my garment is steeped, and cannot find it. » It was his own scent he was chasing at the gallop.
sagesses.commentaire: The more we race after what we seek, the further we drive it off, for it is within us. Agitation is the enemy of finding. Today, before what you lack, try to stop rather than to run. What you seek may already be breathing within your own breath.

sagesses.texte: A wave, seeing the others break upon the shore, was seized with terror. A wiser wave told it: « You are not a wave. You are the ocean, caught for an instant in this form. »
sagesses.commentaire: The fear of ending comes from our taking ourselves for the passing form rather than for the life that runs through it. The wave vanishes; the water remains. Today, before what is ending within you or around you, remember: what bears you up does not break with the form.

sagesses.titre: The Water of the Duck's Water
sagesses.texte: A distant relative gave Nasreddin a duck, of which he made a fine soup. Then came the relative's friends, whom he fed with the same broth. Then the friends of the friends. At last he served them hot water. « What is this? » « It is the soup of the soup of the duck's water. »
sagesses.commentaire: By dint of diluting a true thing, only its name remains. We invoke friendship, tradition, love, until nothing is left but lukewarm water. Today, ask yourself where, in your life, you still serve « the soup of the soup » — a gesture emptied of its meaning. Better a little real broth than a great deal of hot water.

sagesses.texte: A diamond fell into the mud. Passers-by trod upon it. Its worth was not lessened by a single grain; only their eyes did not know how to see.
sagesses.commentaire: What you are worth depends neither on the gaze of others nor on the circumstances that soil you. The mud passes, the diamond remains. When you are misjudged, trampled, belittled, remember that your worth is not haggled over in the marketplace of opinions. Today, entrust to no one the task of setting your price.

sagesses.texte: A child shouted « I hate you! » and the mountain answered him the same. Frightened, he ran to his mother. « Shout your love to it, » she said. He did so, and the mountain sent love back to him.
sagesses.commentaire: The world often returns to us what we cast at it. Our way of looking at beings shapes what they show us in return. Today, before complaining of the harshness around you, listen to the cry you send out. Life is an echo; choose your voice well.

sagesses.texte: A sage kept pulling from the water a scorpion that stung him each time. « Why save it, since it stings you? » « Because to sting is its nature, and to save is mine. Why should I give up mine on account of its own? »
sagesses.commentaire: To remain faithful to one's own light, even when one is wounded, is the true strength. To answer evil with evil is to let the other decide who we are. Today, when someone stings you, ask yourself: shall I become a scorpion, or remain the water that saves?

sagesses.titre: The Dream of a Gold Coin
sagesses.texte: Nasreddin dreamed he was offered nine gold coins; he demanded ten. He was refused. Furious, he woke, saw his hand empty, quickly closed it and said: « All right, give me the nine! »
sagesses.commentaire: By craving too much the surplus, we lose even what was offered us. Contentment is not weakness: it is the art of holding what one has. Today, look at the nine coins of your life — health, a roof, a beloved face — before weeping over the tenth you do not have.

sagesses.texte: Three moths wished to know what the flame was. The first saw it from afar and returned to « speak of light. » The second brushed against it and returned to « speak of heat. » The third entered the fire and did not return: it alone had truly known.
sagesses.commentaire: To know through words is to circle around. To know truly is to let oneself be consumed. There is a knowledge that asks one to give oneself entirely, with no return possible. Today, in what matters most to you, ask yourself: do I speak of it from afar, or do I enter into it?

sagesses.texte: Two monks met a woman blocked by a river. One carried her to the far bank. Hours later, the other, still troubled, reproached him for that forbidden contact. « I set her down on the bank. You are carrying her still. »
sagesses.commentaire: We long carry burdens the moment has already rendered useless: resentments, regrets, words turned over and over. The act is past; only what we refuse to set down remains. Today, ask yourself which « woman » you are still carrying in your thoughts, and set her at last upon the bank.

sagesses.titre: The Star at the Bottom of the Well
sagesses.texte: A man leaned over a well and saw a star within it. He thought it had fallen to the bottom and spent his life trying to draw it out. He had only to lift his eyes.
sagesses.commentaire: We seek below, in our lacks and our sorrows, a light that shines above. The reflection fascinates us and makes us forget the source. Today, if you lean too long over your inner well, raise your head: the sky has never ceased shining above you.

sagesses.texte: The wound is the place where the light enters you.
sagesses.commentaire: We would like a life without crack, smooth and sealed. But a cup without a fissure lets nothing through. What has broken you is perhaps the opening through which something greater seeks to reach you. Today, look at your wound not as a shame to hide, but as a window.

sagesses.texte: What you seek is also seeking you.
sagesses.commentaire: We believe ourselves alone in desiring, alone in reaching out a hand into the void. Yet the impulse that bears you towards the Real is already the trace of the Real within you. Desire is not a lack: it is a call answering a call. Today, in your very thirst, sense the presence that kindled it.

sagesses.texte: Sell your cleverness and buy wonder.
sagesses.commentaire: We are proud to understand everything, to master everything, to calculate everything. But the soul is not nourished by reasonings: it lives on amazement before what surpasses it. Today, before some ordinary thing — a face, a tree, the sky — cease to explain it and simply let yourself be astonished.

sagesses.texte: Yesterday I was clever, and I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, and I am changing myself.
sagesses.commentaire: So long as we wish to set others straight, we remain at the threshold of wisdom. The only transformation truly within our power begins within us. Today, the energy you spend correcting the world, turn it gently back towards your own heart.

sagesses.texte: Silence is the ocean; speech is but a stream of it. When the ocean seeks you, do not run towards the stream.
sagesses.commentaire: We fill our days with words, fearing emptiness as a danger. But it is in silence that things take on their true weight. Today, grant yourself a moment saying and thinking nothing, and listen to what speaks when you fall silent.

sagesses.texte: If I worship Thee out of fear of hell, burn me in hell; if I worship Thee out of desire for paradise, shut paradise against me. But if I worship Thee for Thyself alone, do not deny me Thy beauty.
sagesses.commentaire: To love for the reward is still to love oneself. Rābiʿa calls us to a love that bargains for nothing. Today, ask yourself what good you do: out of fear, out of calculation, or out of pure love? The answer reveals where your heart stands.

sagesses.texte: She was seen running, a torch in one hand, a jug of water in the other. « I want to burn paradise and quench hell, so that God may be loved without hope or fear. »
sagesses.commentaire: So long as promise and threat govern our heart, we are not yet free. Pure love expects nothing and flees nothing. Today, perform an act of kindness no one will see and from which you will draw no profit: taste the savour of love freely given.

sagesses.texte: I sought Thee throughout the whole world, and it was within myself, nearer to me than my jugular vein, that Thou wert standing.
sagesses.commentaire: The longest journey often brings us back to the starting point: ourselves. What we pursue without already dwells within. Today, before seeking elsewhere what you lack, close your eyes a moment and descend towards that silent centre where all things rest.

sagesses.texte: The colour of water is that of its vessel.
sagesses.commentaire: Pure water has no tint of its own: it takes on that of the glass that holds it. So the limpid soul reflects each one without hardening into judgements. Today, before those you meet, try to be that clear water: empty enough of yourself to welcome their colour without betraying it.

sagesses.texte: For thirty years, said Bāyazīd, I believed I was forging myself in the fire of effort. The day I ceased, I understood that it was He, from all eternity, who was the smith.
sagesses.commentaire: We wear ourselves out improving ourselves as if all depended on our will alone. There comes a day when one must let go of the hammer and let oneself be shaped. Today, where you strive most, try to ease your grip: perhaps a more secret work is already accomplishing you.

sagesses.texte: Thirty birds crossed mountains and deserts to find the mysterious King. At the journey's end, in the King's mirror, they saw only thirty birds: themselves.
sagesses.commentaire: The goal of the path was not a distant throne, but the revelation of what they already were. To seek the absolute is to learn slowly to recognise oneself. Today, despise no stage of your road: each trial brings you nearer to the mirror where you will see yourself at last.

sagesses.texte: Cast yourself into the sea, He told me, and if you drown without calling on Me, you are not of Mine.
sagesses.commentaire: Trust is not proved in comfort, but in the fall. So long as we keep hold of a railing, we do not truly surrender ourselves. Today, where fear makes you cling, try a small letting-go: entrust to one greater than yourself what you cannot carry alone.

sagesses.texte: My heart has become capable of every form: a cloister for the monk, a temple for the idols, a meadow for the gazelles. I am the religion of love, wherever its caravans may turn.
sagesses.commentaire: A heart enlarged by love closes itself to no one. The walls we raise between beliefs are often those of our own narrowness. Today, where you would be tempted to exclude in the name of your truth, ask yourself whether love, for its part, would exclude.

sagesses.texte: Abū Saʿīd was asked what Sufism is. He answered: « What you have in your head, abandon it; what you have in your hand, give it; and what befalls you, do not flee from it. »
sagesses.commentaire: Three simple gestures, and a whole lifetime to learn them: to lighten the mind, to open the hand, to welcome the moment. We often do the reverse: we pile up ideas, clench our possessions, flee the ordeal. Today, choose one of the three and practise living it.

sagesses.texte: Even after all this time, the sun never says to the earth: « You owe me something. » See what such a love does: it lights up the whole sky.
sagesses.commentaire: Love that keeps accounts is no longer quite love. To give while awaiting a return is to lend, not to love. Today, offer a light — a smile, a help, a patience — inscribing the debt nowhere. It is thus that the heart grows wider.

sagesses.texte: Knowledge without action is folly, and action without knowledge is vain.
sagesses.commentaire: To know the path does not make one walk; to act without understanding makes one go round in circles. Wisdom weds the two: a light that lights the step, a step that follows the light. Today, take a truth you know well but do not embody, and make of it a single small act.

sagesses.texte: The children of Adam are the limbs of one same body, sprung from one same essence. If one of them suffers, the others cannot remain at rest.
sagesses.commentaire: Indifference is a numbed limb that believes itself severed from the body. Another's pain is never wholly foreign to our own. Today, before someone's grief, do not wait to know them in order to feel it: recognise in them a part of yourself.

sagesses.texte: Whoever knows himself knows his Lord. Yet many men traverse the stars without ever visiting the grain of dust they inhabit.
sagesses.commentaire: We can name the stars and know nothing of our own heart. The greatest of sciences is inward, and the most neglected. Today, grant your inner world the curiosity you ordinarily reserve for the outer: who are you, truly, when no one is watching?

sagesses.texte: Dhū-l-Nūn was asked: « When does one know that one truly loves? » He said: « When one ceases to wonder whether one is loved in return. »
sagesses.commentaire: So long as we measure what is given back to us, we still love for our own sake. Love fulfilled forgets its rights and thinks only of giving. Today, in one of your affections, mark the moment when you start keeping accounts — and give, just this once, expecting nothing.

sagesses.texte: He was asked where tomorrow's bread would come from. The sage answered: « From where it came yesterday, without my knowing. »
sagesses.commentaire: Worry over tomorrow steals today from us without adding anything to tomorrow. He who has been fed thus far can trust for what follows. Today, set down for a moment the care of what you do not control, and simply savour the bread you already have.

sagesses.texte: Shiblī was shut up among the madmen. Visitors came. « Who are you? » « Those who love you. » He began to hurl stones at them. « If you loved me, you would not flee at the first ordeal. »
sagesses.commentaire: Love that flees at the first pebble was only a disguised comfort. To love is to remain even when it costs. Today, ask yourself with what love you love: that of the easy days, or that which holds firm also when you are let down?

sagesses.texte: What use is it to light a lamp in full sunlight? To show how small our light is beside its own.
sagesses.commentaire: Our efforts, our merits, our knowledge are useful lamps in the night. But before the boundless light of the Real, they fade like a flame at morning. Today, be proud of your little lamp without forgetting the sun: humility does not put out, it sets in proportion.

sagesses.texte: One does not eat the shell of the walnut, but the kernel hidden within it. The form is made so that we may go to the heart.
sagesses.commentaire: Rites, words, appearances are shells: precious, but protecting something other than themselves. To stop at the husk is to miss the fruit. Today, in one of your habits — a prayer, a gesture, a routine — seek the kernel it is meant to nourish.

sagesses.texte: If the river sings, it is because of the stones along its way. Water without obstacle would be mute.
sagesses.commentaire: What resists us also makes us sing. A life without any trial would be a flat and voiceless water. Today, before the stone that bars your road, ask yourself what melody it might teach you, instead of merely cursing it.

sagesses.titre: Turning Your Back to the Sun
sagesses.texte: You see your shadow stretched out only when you turn your back to the sun. Face it, and the shadow falls behind you.
sagesses.commentaire: Our fears and our faults grow large when we stare into the darkness. Turned towards the light, they do not vanish, but cease to go before us. Today, rather than scrutinising your shadow, turn towards what gives you light: the rest will follow, behind.

sagesses.texte: The seed does not complain of the darkness where it is buried: it knows that this is where all things begin.
sagesses.commentaire: The dark seasons of our lives resemble a burial, when they are often a germination. What grows has first need of shadow and silence. Today, if you pass through a dark time, see in it not only an end: perhaps a root is preparing itself there.

sagesses.texte: In the same garden, the bee finds the nectar and the fly seeks the filth. Each gathers what it came to find.
sagesses.commentaire: The world is full of flowers and of refuse; our gaze decides what we draw from it. To complain endlessly is to train one's heart to see filth everywhere. Today, practise being a bee: in your day, go first towards what can become honey.

sagesses.texte: The oyster makes its pearl not from what caresses it, but from the grain of sand that wounds it.
sagesses.commentaire: Our finest transformations are often born of an irritation, a lack, a pain. The oyster does not expel its grain: it patiently coats it with mother-of-pearl. Today, what disturbs you, instead of casting it out, ask what pearl it invites you to fashion.

sagesses.texte: The river loses its name on entering the sea; yet it has lost nothing, it has become the sea.
sagesses.commentaire: We hold so dearly to our name, our story, our own form. Yet what we take for a disappearance may be a fulfilment. Today, where you fear to lose yourself — in a love, a service, a surrender — ask whether you are not becoming more vast.

sagesses.texte: The mirror never lies; but our breath veils it, and we blame the mirror.
sagesses.commentaire: The Real shows itself as it is; it is our agitation that clouds it. When the world seems to us ugly or confused, it is often the mist of our moods that covers it over. Today, before judging what you see, take the time to wipe your own gaze.

sagesses.texte: The fruit does not fall because you shake it, but because it is ripe. What is ready lets go of its own accord.
sagesses.commentaire: We force things before their hour: the partings, the departures, the decisions. Yet what is meant to come falls without violence when its time has arrived. Today, where you long to shake the tree, ask yourself whether the fruit is ripe — or whether you can still wait.

sagesses.texte: The traveller was asked why he carried so little. "The road is long," he said, "and at the end one takes only what one is."
sagesses.commentaire: We hoard as though we were meant to stay, when in truth we are only passing through. At the end, nothing of our possessions follows us; only what we have become remains. Today, lighten your load a little — an object, a worry, a grudge — and taste the freedom of the unburdened traveller.

sagesses.texte: The star does not vanish at dawn: it is a greater light that covers it over.
sagesses.commentaire: When a higher joy arrives, our small glimmers fade without dying. To lose one's importance within a vaster presence is no defeat. Today, where your ego wants to shine alone, consent to be the star that the dawn gently overtakes.

sagesses.texte: The closed hand can neither give nor receive. Only the open hand does both.
sagesses.commentaire: By dint of holding back, we close ourselves to what would gladly be offered. The very clench that prevents giving prevents receiving. Today, notice where your hand — outward or inward — stays clenched, and dare to open it, if only a little.

sagesses.texte: The same wind that snuffs out the small flame fans the great fire.
sagesses.commentaire: Trial extinguishes the weak and strengthens the ardent. It is not the wind that decides, but the intensity of the fire. Today, before the difficulty that blows upon you, do not ask that it cease: ask to become a blaze that it can only rouse the higher.

sagesses.texte: The spring does not ask the one who drinks whether he is worthy of the water. It gives because it is a spring.
sagesses.commentaire: We often measure our generosity by the merit of the one who receives. But true goodness gives by its nature, not by judgement. Today, do good to someone without asking whether he deserves it: be a spring, and let the water flow.

sagesses.texte: It is just before the dawn that the night is at its darkest.
sagesses.commentaire: Often the trial intensifies at the very moment it nears its end. The densest darkness is sometimes the herald of the light. Today, if all seems black to you, do not conclude too quickly: the dawn is preparing itself precisely within that darkness.

sagesses.texte: The map is not the journey, and the word "water" has never quenched anyone's thirst.
sagesses.commentaire: We often confuse speaking of a thing with living it. Fine ideas about love or peace are not worth a single real gesture. Today, take a truth that you know how to say well, and ask yourself when you last lived it.

sagesses.texte: Before writing a wounding word, dip your pen in silence; many sentences dissolve there.
sagesses.commentaire: The word once flung does not return, and regret erases nothing. A moment of restraint is often enough to dissolve what would have wounded. Today, before the retort that itches in you, allow a silence: you will see how many words did not deserve to be spoken.

sagesses.texte: The more the tree would rise towards the sky, the deeper its roots must descend into the dark.
sagesses.commentaire: One does not rise without taking root: height and depth grow together. To want the light without accepting the obscure labour is to doom oneself to fall at the first wind. Today, with each aspiration upward, give equal care to your roots: humility, patience, truth.

sagesses.texte: A merchant laid out his pearls at the market. An onlooker gazed at them a long while, then walked off with a shrug: "So many white pebbles, all for nothing." The merchant smiled: "It is not my pearls that are poor."
sagesses.commentaire: The worth of a thing depends on the eye that beholds it. Before the same beauty, one marvels and another grows bored. Today, if something seems worthless to you, ask whether it is not your own gaze that lacks eyes to see.

sagesses.texte: Two men were praying. One, flawless, recited without a single error. The other, unlettered, stammered through his tears. It was asked which of the two prayers had risen. The answer came: "The one that set out from the broken heart."
sagesses.commentaire: Impeccable form may be empty, and a sincere stammer may hold everything. It is not correctness that touches, but the truth of the heart. Today, in what you offer — a word, a gesture, a prayer — tend less to perfection than to sincerity.

sagesses.texte: A pilgrim carried two satchels: in front, the faults of others, which he saw without ceasing; behind, his own, which he never saw. "Reverse your bags," a sage told him, "and your road will change."
sagesses.commentaire: We have a keen eye for the wrongs of others and a blind one for our own. Yet it is the bag behind us that truly concerns us. Today, each time you spot a flaw in another, gently turn the satchel round: that reproach may teach you something about yourself.

sagesses.texte: A king asked for a sentence that would be true in joy as in sorrow. They engraved upon a ring: "This too shall pass." In misfortune he found consolation there; in triumph he found temperance.
sagesses.commentaire: Nothing lasts, neither our sorrows nor our joys, and therein lies a twofold consolation. Misfortune grows lighter when one knows it will pass; success ceases to intoxicate us for the very same reason. Today, before what overwhelms or exalts you, murmur: "This too shall pass," and recover your balance.

sagesses.texte: A disciple asked why the master tried him so. "One heats gold to purify it," said the master, "and coal to burn it. It is for you to know which you are."
sagesses.commentaire: The same trial purifies one and consumes the other, according to what each carries within. The fire does not decide: it reveals. Today, in the difficulty that heats you, choose to be gold: let your dross melt away rather than be reduced to ashes.

sagesses.texte: A cracked pitcher, ashamed to lose its water along the way, apologised to the water-bearer. "Look," he said: "it is on your side of the path that the flowers grow. I sowed there, knowing of your crack."
sagesses.commentaire: What we take for our flaw may become, in loving hands, the source of an unexpected beauty. Our lacks sometimes water what our perfection would never have made flower. Today, look upon your crack not as a shame, but as the passage through which your life waters the world.

sagesses.texte: A disciple wished to flee to the desert to find silence. "True silence," said the master, "is not the absence of noise around you, but the absence of noise within you. Find it first in the heart of the marketplace."
sagesses.commentaire: Fleeing the world is not enough if one carries one's inner tumult along. True calm dwells within, independent of what lies without. Today, in the midst of the bustle, seek that still point within you: it is there, and not in the desert, that silence dwells.

sagesses.texte: Mounted in the pulpit, Nasreddin asked: "Do you know what I am going to say?" "No!" "Then what is the use of speaking." He left. The next time: "Do you know it?" "Yes!" "Then it is pointless." He left again. The third time, half said yes and half said no. "Let those who know explain it to those who do not."
sagesses.commentaire: We want the truth served to us pre-chewed, without seeking anything ourselves. But knowledge is shared, not swallowed whole. Today, rather than waiting to have everything explained to you, pass on the little you know to one who knows less: that is how the light circulates.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin found an eagle and, judging it misshapen, trimmed its hooked beak, filed down its talons, and clipped its wings so that it might resemble a pigeon. "There," he said, well satisfied. "Now you look like a proper bird."
sagesses.commentaire: By insisting that everything fit our norms, we mutilate what was made for the open skies. Difference is not a defect to be corrected. Today, before what towers up in another — or in yourself — resist the urge to trim it: perhaps it is a wing.

sagesses.texte: A scholar was crossing the river by boat with Nasreddin. "Do you know grammar?" "No." "Then you have lost half your life." The boat began to sink. "Do you know how to swim?" "No." "Then you are about to lose all of yours."
sagesses.commentaire: Knowledge that does not know how to live cannot save when the water rises. Many of our learnings swell us without keeping us. Today, among all that you know, ask yourself what would truly make you swim on the day the boat breaks apart.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin sold his house but kept for himself the ownership of a single nail driven into the wall, with the right to hang upon it whatever he wished. Little by little he hung so many things there that the buyer, exasperated, gave him back the house.
sagesses.commentaire: A single attachment retained can be enough to reclaim the whole space. That little "nail" we refuse to release ends up cluttering the entire house of our heart. Today, spot the nail you still cling to, and ask yourself all that it makes you carry.

sagesses.texte: A child wept before the underside of the carpet: knots, tangled threads, no pattern at all. The weaver turned it over: on the other side shone a garden of flowers.
sagesses.commentaire: Of our lives we often see only the underside: knots, crossings-out, dangling threads. The pattern, meanwhile, is composed on a side we cannot yet see. Today, before what seems to you to have neither sense nor beauty, have trust: perhaps you are weaving a garden whose face is still hidden from you.

sagesses.texte: A lion, seeing its reflection in the spring, believed it a rival and leapt to fight it. It drowned attacking an image. The sage drank from the same water, untroubled.
sagesses.commentaire: Many of our enemies are only our own reflections, our fears projected upon the world. In fighting images, one exhausts oneself and drowns. Today, before what seems to attack you, ask whether it is not your own reflection that you take for an adversary.

sagesses.texte: A man sowed in the morning and returned in the evening to dig up the seed, disappointed that it had not sprouted. He did this every day. Nothing ever grew: he was digging up his own patience.
sagesses.commentaire: To check ceaselessly is to prevent what one desires from ripening. The seed asks to be trusted in the dark. Today, where you dig up your effort out of impatience, let the earth do its work instead: some things grow only if they are left alone.

sagesses.texte: A disciple blew out the master's candle to light up a question: "Where does the flame go when one blows it out?" The master relit it, blew it out in turn, and said: "Where does your question go now that it is answered?"
sagesses.commentaire: Where does what is extinguished come from, where does what disappears go? The mind seeks answers where one ought rather to bow before the mystery. Today, keep within you a question without an answer like a precious candle: not all riddles are made to be solved, but to be inhabited.

sagesses.texte: Listen to the reed as it tells its tale: ever since it was cut from the reed-bed, it weeps for the parting.
sagesses.commentaire: Our most secret longing is not for a place, but for an origin. That emptiness which no possession can fill is the sign that we come from elsewhere. Today, do not smother this lack beneath distractions: listen to it as a song that recalls you to your source.

sagesses.texte: When you are cast into the fire, become flame and dance.
sagesses.commentaire: Resisting the trial only deepens the burn. There is another way: to embrace what comes rather than struggle against it. Today, before what you cannot change, cease to stiffen and seek the right movement: sometimes welcoming is the only way not to burn.

sagesses.texte: Beyond the ideas of right-doing and wrong-doing, there is a field. It is there that I shall wait for you.
sagesses.commentaire: Our quarrels are rooted in the need to be right. But love dwells in a place where the reckonings fall silent. Today, in a disagreement, dare to set aside for a moment the question of who is wrong: join the other in that field where, at last, we meet.

sagesses.texte: You are not a drop in the ocean: you are the whole ocean within a drop.
sagesses.commentaire: We think ourselves tiny, lost in the vastness. Yet the whole is reflected in the smallest part. Today, where you feel insignificant, remember that you carry within you something immense, gathered into a single heart.

sagesses.texte: I wish to show you, when you feel lonely or in the dark, the astonishing light of your own being.
sagesses.commentaire: We wait for another to light us up, never seeing the brightness we already carry. Solitude is dark only because we forget to look within. Today, in a moment of emptiness, turn towards that inner glimmer which needs no one in order to shine.

sagesses.texte: Even after all this time, the moon claims no rent for the beauty she lends you each night.
sagesses.commentaire: The beauty of the world is offered to us without a bill. We pass it by, harried, without lifting our eyes. Today, take a moment to receive a free gift — the sky, a fragrance, a light — and let gratitude replace worry.

sagesses.texte: The tree most laden with fruit is the one that bows its branches lowest.
sagesses.commentaire: True inner richness does not stiffen: it inclines. The fuller a heart, the more humble and approachable it becomes. Today, if you know yourself rich in some knowledge or gift, do not brandish it: bow towards those who lack it, as the branch bows towards the hand that gathers.

sagesses.texte: Until a man has spoken, his flaw and his talent remain hidden. Do not trust hollow wood: it betrays itself the moment it is struck.
sagesses.commentaire: Speech unveils what appearance concealed. It is sometimes better to keep silent than to betray one's emptiness with a word too many. Today, before speaking to shine, ask yourself whether your silence might not speak more truly: restraint too is a form of wisdom.

sagesses.texte: If you wish your mirror to stay clear, do not look for the dust on the mirror of others.
sagesses.commentaire: The eye busy with the faults of others goes blind to its own. To polish one's own mirror is a labour without end and without witness. Today, each time you spot a stain on someone else, return to your own reflection: it is there, and there alone, that you can wipe it clean.

sagesses.titre: Everywhere the Face of the Beloved
sagesses.texte: Wherever I turn my gaze, it is His face that I see; upon whatever I rest my eyes, it is His beauty that appears to me.
sagesses.commentaire: To the eye of love, nothing is foreign: everything becomes sign and presence. The world changes its savour according to what the heart seeks within it. Today, practise seeing, in an ordinary face or a commonplace object, the trace of a vaster beauty hidden there.

sagesses.texte: I was a hidden treasure, and I loved to be known: so I created the world to be known.
sagesses.commentaire: If all that exists does so to be recognised and loved, then no being before you is trifling. Every face is a page where the Real lets itself be read. Today, look upon a person or a thing as a secret that longs to be known, and offer it your attention.

sagesses.texte: Rest from the care of governing yourself: what Another has already taken in hand, do not take back upon your own shoulders.
sagesses.commentaire: We wear ourselves out foreseeing everything, as if the universe depended on our vigilance. Part of our fatigue comes from carrying burdens that are not ours. Today, set down a worry you cannot master, and taste what it means to trust.

sagesses.texte: Let the delay of a gift, despite the insistence of your asking, not drive you to despair: it is promised to you according to what suits you, not according to what you choose.
sagesses.commentaire: Delay is not always a refusal: sometimes it is a care. What tarries ripens us to receive it, or spares us what we do not know to be dangerous. Today, before a prayer left unanswered, replace bitterness with patience: the right hour is not always our own.

sagesses.texte: When a cloud hides the sun from you, do not accuse the sun of having gone out.
sagesses.commentaire: Our inner veils make us believe the light has vanished, when it has never ceased to shine. The eclipse is in us, not in the source. Today, if the presence seems absent to you, do not conclude that it has left you: seek rather which cloud is passing before your heart.

sagesses.texte: Al-Junayd was asked how to find water. He said: "Begin by being truly thirsty."
sagesses.commentaire: We seek well only what we truly need. As long as our desire stays lukewarm, no answer fulfils us. Today, rather than accumulating answers, let a true thirst grow within you: it is this that will lead you to the spring.

sagesses.texte: His austerities were being praised. Bāyazīd replied: "A dog has more merit than I: it fasts without ever believing itself a saint."
sagesses.commentaire: The subtlest of snares is to feel oneself virtuous. Pride slips even into our purest efforts. Today, do a good deed and then forget it at once: the true poverty of the heart is to keep no ledger of one's own merits.

sagesses.texte: She was asked whether she loved the Prophet. "I love him," said Rābiʿa, "but love of the Creator has so filled me that there remains no room to love or hate any other."
sagesses.commentaire: When the heart overflows with the essential, the rest naturally finds its place. This is not indifference, but order: to love the source is to love all that flows from it. Today, ask yourself what occupies the centre of your heart — for all the rest arranges itself around it.

sagesses.texte: Upon his door was written: "Whoever enters here, give him bread without questioning him on his faith: whoever deserves life beside the Real surely deserves bread beside me."
sagesses.commentaire: True generosity does not ask the hungry heart for its papers. Before judging who deserves our kindness, let us remember that life itself makes no such sorting. Today, offer something to someone without first checking whether he is one of your own.

sagesses.texte: Keep company with one whose mere presence recalls you to your source, and whose deeds, more than his words, invite you to the best.
sagesses.commentaire: We become, little by little, those we keep company with. An upright presence sets us straight without a word; a troubled one erodes us before we notice. Today, look at who surrounds you and ask: does this company draw me closer to who I wish to be?

sagesses.texte: There is not a leaf that trembles, not a drop that falls, that does not celebrate, in its own mute way, the One who made it.
sagesses.commentaire: The world prays without words, and we remain deaf to it, cluttered with our own noise. To learn to hear that silent song is to join an immense praise already underway. Today, before a detail of nature, fall silent for a moment: perhaps you will hear it celebrate.

sagesses.texte: As long as you believe yourself to be something, the door stays shut. Become nothing, and you will pass through without even seeing it.
sagesses.commentaire: Our self-importance is precisely what blocks us at the threshold. The ego swells and no longer fits; the humble one effaces himself and passes through. Today, where you knock against a wall, ask whether it is not your sense of being someone that will not fit through the opening.

sagesses.texte: Shiblī was reproached for his disjointed words. He said: "My words are the pearls of a necklace that love has broken: gather them up if you can."
sagesses.commentaire: What springs from the heart does not always have the order of discourse. Sometimes truth speaks itself in fragments that reason struggles to re-thread. Today, do not scorn a word merely because it is clumsy: seek the pearl beneath the disorder.

sagesses.texte: Sahl was asked what his secret was. He said: "I do not swallow a single mouthful without remembering Who gave it to me."
sagesses.commentaire: The secret lies not in great feats, but in an attentiveness. To live each ordinary gesture in presence transfigures it. Today, eat a single meal slowly, conscious of the gift it represents: you will discover that the commonplace can become sacred.

sagesses.texte: He who knows without living what he knows is like the donkey that carries a library: the load crushes him without ever enlightening him.
sagesses.commentaire: Knowledge unlived weighs instead of carrying. One may know everything of the paths and never walk. Today, choose a single truth you have carried for a long time without embodying, and turn it into a real step, however small.

sagesses.titre: To Die in Order to Live
sagesses.texte: Kill me, my faithful friends, for in my death is my life, and my life is in my death.
sagesses.commentaire: There is a death that is not an end: that of the ego opening to something greater than itself. What we let go of ourselves is not lost, but transformed. Today, let a small pride die, an old image of yourself: you will see that one can be reborn from what one relinquishes.

sagesses.titre: The Wider the Vision, the Fewer the Words
sagesses.texte: The wider the vision grows, the narrower the expression becomes.
sagesses.commentaire: What is immense does not fit within language: before the most true, words fall silent. Chatter is often the sign that one has not truly seen. Today, before a deep emotion or beauty, resist the urge to comment upon it: let silence keep its grandeur.

sagesses.titre: The Sufi Is Like the Earth
sagesses.texte: The Sufi is like the earth: all manner of filth is cast upon it, and from it grow only flowers.
sagesses.commentaire: To receive evil without returning it, and to transform it into beauty: this is an alchemy of the heart. The earth does not insult the one who soils it; it converts. Today, when ill humour or reproach is poured upon you, try to be earth: keep what may serve, and make something good grow from it.

sagesses.texte: Two wolves are fighting within you: one made of anger and fear, the other of peace and goodness. Which one wins? The one you feed.
sagesses.commentaire: Our thoughts are not fates: what we feed grows, what we neglect weakens. To brood over a grudge is to hold out the meat to the wrong wolf. Today, watch what you give your attention to: meal by meal, you choose which of the two prevails.

sagesses.texte: "My life is bitter," a man complained. The sage had him throw a handful of salt into a glass: undrinkable. Then into the lake: the water stayed sweet. "Widen your heart, and the same sorrow will become bearable."
sagesses.commentaire: The pain does not change, but the space that receives it does. A narrow heart makes a poison of a grain of salt; a vast heart dissolves it. Today, before your sorrow, do not seek first to remove it: seek to widen the vessel that holds it.

sagesses.texte: If life draws you like a bow and pulls you backwards, do not complain: it is because it is about to send you far ahead.
sagesses.commentaire: What makes us draw back sometimes prepares our greatest surge forward. The retreat is not always a fall: it can be a drawing of the bow. Today, where you feel pulled backwards, thwarted in your advance, ask towards which target you might just be aimed.

sagesses.texte: Before speaking, pass your words through three sieves: is it true? is it good? is it useful? If nothing remains, keep silent.
sagesses.commentaire: So many of our words are neither true, nor good, nor useful: pure noise that soils the air. Silence is worth more than a word that passes through none of the three sieves. Today, before a word that itches in you, pass it through these three sieves: you will speak less, and better.

sagesses.texte: Seeing the butterfly struggle to leave the cocoon, a man helped it by cutting the silk. The insect emerged, but never flew: it was the effort of leaving that was meant to swell its wings.
sagesses.commentaire: To spare every hardship to the one we love can deprive him of the strength that is born of effort. Some struggles are not misfortunes but births. Today, before the one you wish to rescue, ask whether your help sets him free, or whether it clips his wings.

sagesses.texte: It was asked why the rich man, behind his pane, saw only his own reflection, while the poor man, behind his, saw the passers-by. "Add a little silver to the glass," said the sage, "and the window becomes a mirror."
sagesses.commentaire: A little silver on the back of the glass, and one sees only oneself. Wealth, taken too seriously, endlessly throws back our own image. Today, mentally scrape away that silver which turns you into a mirror: become a pane again, and watch others pass by at last.

sagesses.texte: To fill the vessel of your life, put in the big stones first; the sand will always find its place between them. Begin with the sand, and no stone will fit any more.
sagesses.commentaire: Our days fill up with dust, and the essential has no more room. The order in which we welcome things decides everything. Today, place your big stones first — what truly matters — and let the small things slip into the gaps.

sagesses.texte: For years the bamboo shows nothing: it digs its roots. Then, in a few weeks, it shoots up higher than all the trees.
sagesses.commentaire: The time when nothing appears is not lost time: it is the time of the invisible rooting. We would have fruit before we have roots. Today, if your efforts seem to produce nothing, do not lose heart: perhaps you are preparing, in silence, a growth that nothing will stop.

sagesses.texte: Fallen to the bottom of the well, the donkey, at each shovelful thrown down to bury him, shook the earth from his back and climbed upon it. What was meant to bury him brought him back up.
sagesses.commentaire: What life throws upon us can crush us or raise us, depending on what we make of it. The same earth buries the one who gives up and bears the one who shakes himself free. Today, beneath whatever falls upon you, do not stay still: shake it off, and turn it into a foothold.

sagesses.texte: They wanted to throw away the cracked bell. "Keep it," said the sage: "its broken voice touches hearts where the perfect bell merely resounds."
sagesses.commentaire: Our flaws can become passages towards others. What is too smooth slides off; what is marked catches and consoles. Today, do not be ashamed of your crack: it is often through it that your voice reaches another's pain.

sagesses.texte: The gardener was asked how to keep the weeds away. "I do not chase them off: I plant so many flowers that no room is left for them."
sagesses.commentaire: We do not empty the heart of its shadows by fighting them head-on: we replace them by cultivating what is good. Light drives out darkness better than any stick. Today, rather than struggling against a fault, plant the opposite virtue beside it and let it take the place.

sagesses.texte: As for wounds, write them on the sand, that the wind may erase them; as for kindnesses, carve them in stone, that no wind may carry them away.
sagesses.commentaire: We do the opposite: we engrave offences and forget kindnesses. Our memory chooses what weighs us down. Today, turn over your two ledgers: let the wind take a grudge, and keep preciously a kindness received, however small.

sagesses.titre: The glass of water you hold
sagesses.texte: This glass of water is not heavy. But hold it at arm's length for an hour, and it becomes unbearable. The weight does not come from the glass: it comes from how long you hold it.
sagesses.commentaire: Our worries do not crush us by their size, but by the time we carry them without setting them down. To take up a concern in the morning and keep it until evening makes it heavier. Today, learn to set down your glass: take it up again later if you wish, but grant yourself moments when your hands are free.

sagesses.texte: "What are the people of this town like?" the traveller asked. "What were those of the place you come from?" "Hard and closed." "You will find them the same here." To the next who called them good, the sage replied: "You will find them good here too."
sagesses.commentaire: We carry everywhere the gaze that decides what we meet. The world is not so different from one place to another: it is the eye that changes. Today, before judging a new place or a new face, remember that you often project your own weather.

sagesses.texte: If your rope does not reach the water, do not accuse the well of being empty.
sagesses.commentaire: When meaning escapes us, we conclude too quickly that there is none. Often it is our instrument that is too short, not the source that is lacking. Today, faced with what you do not understand, ask yourself first whether it is not your rope that needs lengthening.

sagesses.texte: To hold on to resentment is to seize a burning coal to hurl at another: it is your own hand that burns first.
sagesses.commentaire: The hatred we believe we aim at another consumes us first. We poison ourselves in the hope of striking the other. Today, where you clutch an old grievance, open your hand: to let go of the coal is not to forgive out of weakness, it is to stop burning yourself.

sagesses.texte: The bird perched on a fragile branch is not afraid that the branch will give way: its trust is not in the branch, but in its wings.
sagesses.commentaire: Our security comes not from outer support, ever fragile, but from what we carry within. The one who counts on his wings does not tremble when the branch bends. Today, where some support threatens to give way, recall what, within you, can fly when all else fails.

sagesses.texte: A lamp of gleaming glass but without oil lights nothing. Outward beauty does not kindle the inner flame.
sagesses.commentaire: One can tend the appearance to the utmost and remain inwardly dark. What truly gives light is fed in secret, far from sight. Today, ask yourself whether you polish above all the glass of your life, or whether you take care to pour in the oil that makes the flame shine.

sagesses.titre: The knot at the end of the thread
sagesses.texte: Without a knot at the end of the thread, the first stitch makes it slip and everything comes undone. Learn to tie the knot: commit yourself.
sagesses.commentaire: Many of our efforts come undone for want of an anchoring point: we begin without binding ourselves. Commitment is the knot that holds the work fast. Today, where you hesitate to truly commit, tie the knot: without it, even your finest gestures slip away and are lost.

sagesses.texte: It is not the mountains that exhaust the walker, but the grain of sand in his shoe.
sagesses.commentaire: Great trials often find us standing; it is the small repeated annoyances that wear us down. We face the heroic and stumble over the tiny. Today, spot your grain of sand — a habit, a daily irritation — and take the time to remove it.

sagesses.titre: The water that wears the rock
sagesses.texte: It is not by its force that water pierces the rock, but by its constancy.
sagesses.commentaire: We believe it takes great blows to transform what is hard. Yet the gentleness that returns without ceasing overcomes what violence cannot dent. Today, faced with a resistance — in yourself or in another — choose patient constancy rather than force that wears itself out.

sagesses.titre: The dead sea and the living lake
sagesses.texte: The same river feeds two waters: one receives and gives back, its waters teem with life; the other receives and keeps everything, its waters are dead and salt.
sagesses.commentaire: To receive without ever giving ends by corrupting everything, even abundance. Life flows, or else it stagnates. Today, ask yourself whether you are a lake that lets it flow or a sea that holds back: what you receive — time, love, goods — is alive only if it sets off again towards others.

sagesses.texte: To shield a flame from the wind, one does not shut it away: one shelters it while still leaving it the air it lives on.
sagesses.commentaire: To want to protect too much what we love can smother it like a flame under a sealed jar. True care shelters without imprisoning. Today, for what you cherish — a child, a project, a bond — seek the right measure: shelter enough to preserve it, air enough that it may live.

sagesses.texte: A proud king promised to fill the dervish's bowl. But however much they poured in the gold of the whole kingdom, the little bowl stayed empty: it was made from a human skull. "There is your desire," said the dervish: "nothing can fill it."
sagesses.commentaire: Bottomless desire swallows all without ever being sated. One may possess kingdoms and remain inwardly starved. Today, faced with the craving for "just a little more," ask yourself whether your bowl is not broken: contentment alone, not abundance, can fill it.

sagesses.texte: "Where is your prayer mat?" they asked the sage who was helping a wounded man on the road. "Under my feet," he said: "all the ground where good is done is a place of prayer."
sagesses.commentaire: We think worship has a place and an hour, and that elsewhere the profane begins. But a gesture of kindness is already a prayer in act. Today, do not separate the sacred too sharply from the everyday: each service rendered can be a mat unrolled beneath your steps.

sagesses.titre: Lighting a lamp for the neighbour
sagesses.texte: A woman, troubled by the darkness of the street, lit a lamp at her window each evening. It lit her own threshold as much as that of the passers-by.
sagesses.commentaire: The good we do for others lights our own door first. In seeking to serve, we find ourselves served. Today, set a small light for others — a word, a quiet help — without calculation: you will see that your threshold too is the less dark for it.

sagesses.titre: The two uses of the knife
sagesses.texte: The same knife, in the surgeon's hand, saves; in the murderer's, kills. The tool does not decide: it is the heart that holds it.
sagesses.commentaire: Nothing is good or bad in itself: all depends on the intention that wields it. Speech, money, knowledge heal or wound according to the hand. Today, look at some power you hold, however small, and ask yourself with what heart you hold it.

sagesses.texte: "What use is reading if I forget everything?" said the child. The sage had him carry water in a basket full of holes. The water ran out, but the basket, little by little, grew clean. "That is what reading does to you."
sagesses.commentaire: What passes through us transforms us, even when we keep no precise memory of it. Good words wash us without our knowing. Today, do not be discouraged at forgetting what you receive: the essential is not to retain everything, but to let yourself be cleansed.

sagesses.texte: A caged parrot charged a merchant to ask the parrots of the forest how to free himself. There, at the question, a bird fell down stiff. On his return, at this account, the caged parrot fell the same way — and the merchant, believing him dead, opened the cage: at once he flew away.
sagesses.commentaire: To leave his prison, he had to die to what held him: his need to please, his role as the fine bird on display. Freedom sometimes passes through a renunciation that resembles a death. Today, ask yourself to what role you must die so that your cage may open at last.

sagesses.texte: The clay complained of being kneaded, struck, cast into the fire. Once it had become a cup, it saw the water it could offer and the face that drank from it. "Now I know," it said, "why you worked me so hard."
sagesses.commentaire: What shapes us hurts us at the time, and we grasp its meaning only afterwards. The finished form justifies the kneading. Today, beneath a trial that strikes and burns you, hold on to the thought that perhaps some hands are giving you a form able, one day, to quench another's thirst.

sagesses.texte: The runner is not made to keep the torch, but to carry it a while and pass it, still burning, to the one who follows.
sagesses.commentaire: What we receive — a knowledge, a faith, a love — is not ours to keep, but to pass on. To want to hold it is to put it out. Today, ask yourself what torch you carry, and to whom you might already hold it out.

sagesses.texte: Before answering anger, count to ten; if the anger is great, count to a hundred. The time you spend counting is the time in which you do not wound.
sagesses.commentaire: Anger wants an immediate reply, and it is in that instant that one commits the irreparable. A short delay is often enough to deflate what seemed enormous. Today, when you feel the retort rising, grant yourself that small interval: it separates the word one regrets from the silence one blesses.

sagesses.texte: The frog in the well judged the sky to be as large as the opening above her. Out on the meadow, she understood how small her world had been.
sagesses.commentaire: We take the edges of our experience for the limits of the world. What we have never seen, we believe does not exist. Today, faced with some neat certainty about "how the world goes," remember the frog: perhaps you see only a fragment of sky.

sagesses.texte: This world is a bridge: cross it, but do not build your house upon it.
sagesses.commentaire: We settle into the provisional as though it were final, and weigh ourselves down with what we cannot carry away. The bridge is made for crossing, not for dwelling. Today, without scorning life, hold it with a light hand: you are passing through, and that makes each thing precious rather than burdensome.

sagesses.texte: "Open this seed. What do you see?" "Nothing." "It is from that invisible nothing that the immense tree comes forth."
sagesses.commentaire: The essential escapes the gaze: what bears life cannot be dissected. The most powerful hides in the most subtle, almost in the void. Today, do not scorn what you can neither see nor grasp within you: it is perhaps from that nothing that all which matters grows.

sagesses.texte: The one who scatters his path with blessings need not look back: he already walks in a wake of light.
sagesses.commentaire: The good we sow first perfumes our own road. To wish others the best transforms our gaze before it reaches them. Today, in thought, bless those you meet rather than judging them: you will walk in a gentler world, one you yourself have softened.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin borrowed a cauldron and returned it with a small one inside: "Your cauldron has given birth." Delighted, the neighbour accepted. The next time, he did not return it at all. "Alas, your cauldron has died." "A cauldron, die? Impossible!" "You readily believed it could give birth."
sagesses.commentaire: We gladly believe the news that suits us, and reject what costs us, without consistency. Greed makes us credulous, then bitter. Today, faced with good news too good to be true, ask yourself whether you accept it only because it profits you.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin saw the moon fallen to the bottom of the well. He threw down a rope fitted with a hook, pulled with all his might, and tumbled over backwards. From there, he caught sight of the moon in the sky. "What an effort! But at least I have put it back in its place."
sagesses.commentaire: We wear ourselves out solving problems we have imagined, then congratulate ourselves on having "solved" them. The reflection was not the moon. Today, faced with a fierce struggle, first make sure you are not fighting a mere reflection at the bottom of your well.

sagesses.texte: "Hodja, what happens after death?" "I do not know." "What? You, a sage, do not know?" "A sage, perhaps, but not dead."
sagesses.commentaire: To admit one's ignorance is sometimes the highest wisdom. We fill mysteries with hollow answers so as not to face the "I do not know." Today, before a question that surpasses you, dare to say "I do not know": that admission is worth more than a hundred invented certainties.

sagesses.titre: The pot of yoghurt in the lake
sagesses.texte: Nasreddin was seen pouring a spoonful of yoghurt starter into the lake. "You will never curdle a whole lake!" "I know. But imagine for a moment that it works..."
sagesses.commentaire: A part of us knows the undertaking is unreasonable, and yet hope still tempts us to it. This folly is not always foolish: it is what dares the improbable. Today, without lying to yourself about the odds, keep a small place for that "and what if it works" which moves the world forward.

sagesses.texte: Each day, Nasreddin crossed the border with donkeys loaded with straw, searched in vain. Years later, the retired customs officer begged him: "Tell me at last what you were smuggling." "Donkeys."
sagesses.commentaire: We search everywhere for the essential except in the most obvious place, too taken up with the details to see the whole. The secret was before the officer's eyes each day. Today, in a search that keeps defeating you, ask yourself whether the answer is not in what you look at without seeing.

sagesses.texte: Lost on the road at night, Nasreddin let go of his donkey's reins. "What are you doing?" "He knows where the stable is, and I have forgotten. Sometimes, one must follow what is humbler than oneself."
sagesses.commentaire: When our knowledge fails us, it is wise to trust a knowing simpler than our own: instinct, the body, silence. To want to direct everything by the head sometimes leads us astray. Today, where you are lost, try to let go of the reins and follow what, within you, knows the way.

sagesses.texte: Two men in dispute came before Nasreddin, the judge. To the first he said: "You are right." To the second, who pleaded the contrary: "You are right." His wife protested: "They cannot both be right!" "You are right too."
sagesses.commentaire: Each point of view holds its share of truth, and the sage acknowledges it without shutting himself within it. To decide too quickly is often to mutilate the real. Today, in a conflict, try first to hear what is true on each side, before seeking who is wrong.

sagesses.texte: Poorly dressed, Nasreddin was treated with disdain at the public bath, yet he left a generous tip. The next time, he was welcomed like a prince; he left a tiny coin. "The large tip was for this welcome; the small one, for the welcome of last time."
sagesses.commentaire: We often adjust our respect to the supposed rank of the other, and our service to what we hope to gain in return. The sage overturns this calculation to lay it bare. Today, treat with the same dignity the one from whom you expect nothing and the one from whom you hope for everything.

sagesses.texte: Nasreddin was asked why he had cut a large hole for his big cat and a small one beside it for the kitten. "The big cat will not pass through the small hole!" "Of course. But the small one — will it not pass through the large?"
sagesses.commentaire: We complicate through excess of foresight what simplicity would settle at a stroke. By wanting to plan everything in detail, we multiply useless holes. Today, faced with a solution you are splitting endlessly, ask yourself whether a single, wider opening would not suffice.

sagesses.titre: Wake me if I am asleep
sagesses.texte: "Hodja, how do you know whether you are awake or dreaming?" Nasreddin thought, then slapped himself. "Now I know: if it had hurt less, I would have thought I was dreaming."
sagesses.commentaire: It is often pain that pulls us from the sleep of our illusions. We pass through our days half-drowsing, until a jolt wakes us. Today, rather than fleeing what pinches you, ask yourself whether this trial does not come, in its rough way, to wake you.

sagesses.texte: The whole village gathered to pray for rain. Nasreddin looked on and said: "None of you truly believes it will come." "How do you know?" "No one has brought an umbrella."
sagesses.commentaire: True faith is measured by the acts it makes us do, not by the words we utter. To ask without preparing anything is to doubt in secret. Today, what you say you hope for or believe, ask yourself whether you act accordingly: have you brought your umbrella?

sagesses.texte: An innkeeper demanded money from a poor man who had breathed in the smell of his dishes. Nasreddin, called to judge, jingled a few coins in his hand. "There is your payment: the sound of money for the smell of the dishes."
sagesses.commentaire: To an absurd demand, the sage answers with an exact measure: wind for wind. One owes no more than one has received. Today, faced with an unreasonable demand, do not let yourself be made to feel guilty: to the shadow of a service corresponds only the shadow of a debt.

sagesses.texte: A passer-by saw Nasreddin sawing the branch on which he sat. "You will fall!" Nasreddin shrugged. He fell. Marvelling at the prediction, he ran after the man: "You know the future! Tell me when I shall die."
sagesses.commentaire: We take for prophecy what is merely the plain consequence of our acts. Rather than listening to simple wisdom, we chase after soothsayers. Today, before seeking far off the one who will tell you your future, look at which branch you may be sawing through.

sagesses.texte: A hermit was praying under the moon when a thief sprang up. The hermit offered him everything, then added: "Come back tomorrow, I did not know how to give you what matters." Years later, the thief, become a disciple, would say: "That night, he gave me a gaze in which I was not a thief."
sagesses.commentaire: To see in another better than he believes himself to be can make him become that gaze. We often shut people within their worst moments. Today, faced with someone you have labelled, offer him a gaze that frees him from the image you hold of him: you may be giving him a path.

sagesses.texte: A king was searching: what is the right moment? who is the most important person? what is the best action? A hermit answered him by his very life: the right moment is now, the important person is the one before you, the best action is the good you do for him.
sagesses.commentaire: We seek distant answers to questions whose key life always holds in the present instant. The only time in which we can act is now; the only person to love is the one who is here. Today, bring your attention back to this concrete present: it is there that everything is at stake.

sagesses.texte: The farmer's horse ran off. "What misfortune!" "Perhaps." It came back with wild horses. "What luck!" "Perhaps." The son, taming them, broke his leg. "What misfortune!" "Perhaps." The army came to enlist the able young men; the lame son was left behind.
sagesses.commentaire: We judge events good or bad too quickly, when their meaning reveals itself only with time. What looks like a loss sometimes prepares a deliverance, and the reverse. Today, faced with what seems to you misfortune or windfall, keep in reserve this simple word: "perhaps."

sagesses.texte: An ascetic, proud of having learned in thirty years to walk on water, showed it to a master. "How many years?" "Thirty." "What a waste: the ferryman crosses for a small coin."
sagesses.commentaire: One may devote a life to feats that add nothing to love or to peace. The extraordinary seduces the ego, but does not nourish the heart. Today, faced with what would earn you admiration, ask yourself whether it makes you more loving — or only more remarkable.

sagesses.texte: A woodcutter was cutting less and less wood, yet striking harder and harder. "When did you last sharpen your axe?" "I have no time: I am too busy cutting."
sagesses.commentaire: By dint of acting without stopping to renew ourselves, we grow blunt and work at a loss. Rest, prayer, silence are not stolen time: they sharpen. Today, instead of striking harder at a task that resists, take a moment to hone your blade.

sagesses.texte: A beggar died at the roadside where he had begged for thirty years. As they tore open his old coat, they found, sewn into the lining, gold enough for a life of kings.
sagesses.commentaire: We beg outside for what we carry, unknowing, close to us. Inner wealth often sleeps sewn into our ordinary days. Today, before begging joy or meaning elsewhere, feel the lining of your own life: the treasure may already be there.

sagesses.texte: A powerful elephant stayed tied by a thin rope he could have snapped with a single move. "Why does he not run off?" "As a young one, he pulled and could not: he still believes the rope holds him."
sagesses.commentaire: The beliefs forged in our past weaknesses chain us long after we have grown strong. The rope is in the head, not on the feet. Today, spot an "I cannot" that dates from far back, and try: perhaps the rope gave way long ago.

sagesses.texte: Two brothers shared a harvest. Each, by night, secretly carried sheaves to the other's pile, believing him the more in need. The piles stayed equal. One night, they met, sheaves in their arms, and understood.
sagesses.commentaire: True love wants the good of the other in silence, without claiming recognition. When each cares for the other, abundance flows without being counted. Today, do a secret good for someone, without their knowing it came from you: that is where generosity becomes pure.

sagesses.texte: A blind man carried a lantern at night. "What use is the light to you, who cannot see?" "It does not help me to see, but to be seen: so that no one collides with me in the dark."
sagesses.commentaire: What we offer is not always for ourselves: the light we carry can protect others from our own shadows. Kindness wards off collisions only it sees coming. Today, carry a lantern not to shine, but to spare others from colliding with you in their night.

sagesses.texte: The master gave the disciple a bag of salt and a bag of sugar, mixed on the ground. "Separate them." Before the impossible task, the disciple understood: "Better not to mix them." "There it is: watch what you let enter you, for afterwards, it can no longer be untangled."
sagesses.commentaire: What we let penetrate our heart — images, words, company — soon mingles within it beyond extracting. The sorting is done at the entrance, not after. Today, be attentive to what you let in: later it will be impossible to separate it out.

sagesses.texte: An old man was planting a walnut tree. "You will never see its fruit." "My ancestors planted trees whose fruit I have eaten; I plant for those who will come."
sagesses.commentaire: To live only for what one will harvest oneself shrinks existence to a single life. To receive from a past one has not known commits one towards a future one will not see. Today, do a gesture you will not profit from: plant for those who will come, as others planted for you.

sagesses.texte: A candle wondered where its own light came from. It leaned to look within itself and went out. The flame of the sun, for its part, never turns back upon itself: that is why it does not go out.
sagesses.commentaire: To withdraw too much into oneself, to scrutinise one's inwardness endlessly, can put out the very impulse that makes us live. True light gives itself rather than contemplating itself. Today, instead of analysing yourself without end, turn your flame outward: it is by giving light that you will burn best.

sagesses.texte: A sage warned the king: the harvest will drive mad whoever eats it. "But we have no other grain." "Then let us eat of it to survive," said the king, "but let us mark our foreheads, so that at least we may know we are mad."
sagesses.commentaire: When the going astray becomes universal, the only recourse is to keep aware that one is straying. To know one is caught in a common madness is to remain a little free of it.
