Today, faced with the shared frenzies around you, keep this mark on the forehead: the lucidity that does not let itself be quite carried away.

sagesses.texte: A stream wished to cross the desert, but the sand drank its waters. The wind said to it: "Let me carry you." "But I shall no longer be myself!" "You will become rain again on the other side, more a stream than before." The stream evaporated, trusting, and fell again as a torrent beyond the sands.
sagesses.commentaire: Some crossings require that one give up one's form so as not to be wholly lost. To rise, to let oneself be transformed, is not to betray oneself: it is sometimes the only way to go on. Today, faced with an obstacle that drinks your strength, ask yourself whether you must change your state rather than wear yourself out against it.

sagesses.titre: The lesson of the remaining salt
sagesses.texte: The master asked how much salt remained after putting some into the water. "It has disappeared." "Taste." "It is salty." "So it is with what you give: invisible, yet it has changed all the water."
sagesses.commentaire: The good we do sometimes disappears from our eyes and seems to vanish. Yet, like the salt, it has transformed the whole without leaving a visible trace. Today, if you doubt that your efforts serve any purpose, remember the salt: what seems dissolved is still at work.

sagesses.texte: A rich man reproached the dervish for giving to the poor the little he received. "Keep it for tomorrow!" "Why keep for an uncertain tomorrow what can surely serve today?"
sagesses.commentaire: By hoarding for an uncertain future, we let slip the certain good of the present. Fear of the morrow renders today's generosity barren. Today, what you could offer now, do not set aside "just in case": the only day on which you can give is this one.

sagesses.texte: "Master, how many years to reach wisdom?" "Ten years." "And if I redouble my efforts?" "Then twenty." "And if I work day and night without rest?" "Thirty." "Why more, the harder I strive?" "He who always keeps one eye on the goal leaves only one for the road."
sagesses.commentaire: Impatience fixed on the result draws us away from the instant which alone moves us forward. To want to arrive too quickly is to walk badly. Today, in a quest that matters to you, take your gaze a little off the goal and set it on the step you are taking now: it is this that bears you.

sagesses.texte: A blind man asked what colour milk was. "White like a swan." He felt the swan. "So milk has wings and a long neck?" The more they described, the further he strayed. The sage said to him at last: "Stop trying to describe it: one day, perhaps, you will see."
sagesses.commentaire: Some realities cannot be caught by words: by dint of defining them, we deform them. Sometimes one must give up understanding in order to make oneself ready to see. Today, faced with a mystery that explanations do not light up, accept not grasping it, and stay open to what might reveal it to you.

sagesses.titre: The sage and the scorpion at the ford
sagesses.texte: A sage carried an exhausted traveller on his back to cross the ford. Halfway across, the other began to insult him. The sage went on, set him down on the far bank, greeted him and left. "Why did you answer nothing?" he was asked. "Because I was already carrying his body: was I to carry his words as well?"
sagesses.commentaire: One can choose not to pick up the offences hurled at us, as one does not pick up a gift one refuses. What you do not take stays with the one who threw it. Today, faced with a wounding word, ask yourself whether you are obliged to carry it: often, it is enough to let it fall.

sagesses.titre: The Master and the Bowl of Water
sagesses.texte: A hot-tempered disciple came seeking counsel. The master filled a bowl with muddy water and set it down. "Come back when you can see clearly through it." After a while the mud had settled and the water was limpid. "You did nothing, and yet it cleared. So too with your anger, if you stop stirring it up."
sagesses.commentaire: Our troubled emotions do not clear by being stirred, but by being left to settle. To act in the grip of anger is to churn up the mud. Today, when everything within you is murky, decide nothing: set down the bowl, wait, and let the mud sink back of its own accord.

sagesses.texte: Come, come, whoever you are. Wanderer, idolater, lover of leaving, come. Ours is not the door of despair. Though you have broken your vows a hundred times, come yet again.
sagesses.commentaire: No fault exhausts the mercy of the One who calls. The spiritual door keeps no tally of our backslidings: it opens at our slightest turn toward home. In your life, the invitation remains laid open after every failure; it is not perfection that qualifies you, but the act of returning.

sagesses.texte: I died as mineral and became plant. I died as plant and rose to animal. I died as animal and was made man. What should I fear? When was I ever diminished by dying?
sagesses.commentaire: Each death did nothing but strip away a form grown too narrow for what was growing beneath. What you call loss is often the threshold of a vaster degree. Learn to read your own ruptures not as endings but as sheddings.

sagesses.texte: Sell your cleverness and buy bewilderment. Cleverness is mere opinion; bewilderment is vision.
sagesses.commentaire: The calculating intellect wants to reduce everything to what it already knows; wonder, by contrast, consents to the unknown. Before the mystery, to know too much becomes a veil. Keep within you that childlike part still able to be seized before it has understood.

sagesses.texte: Stop taking yourself for the droplet: within you the whole ocean lies hidden.
sagesses.commentaire: The soul shrinks when it identifies with its own outlines. Yet what gazes at you from within has no edge. Stop begging for the crumbs of a world, when the feast already dwells in your breast.

sagesses.texte: Until you have burned, you will never know what light is.
sagesses.commentaire: The knowledge born of love is not information: it is a combustion. One does not explain the flame to the wax; one makes the wax undergo it. What in you resists melting is precisely what keeps you in the dark.

sagesses.texte: Do not go seeking spring outside: the orchard blossoms within you.
sagesses.commentaire: The distracted soul believes that beauty will come to it from landscapes, encounters, acquisitions. But nothing external awakens what does not already lie dormant in seed within. Water the inner root, and the whole world will seem to bloom again.

sagesses.texte: Silence is the language of God; all the rest is but a poor translation.
sagesses.commentaire: Words define; silence welcomes. What is truest in us passes not through the mouth but through presence. Learn to keep silent long enough to hear what speaks beneath your speech.

sagesses.texte: Lovers do not meet one day in some place: they have been within one another from the beginning.
sagesses.commentaire: The separation we mourn is an optical illusion of the ego, which believes itself shut up within its own skin. On the plane of the soul, the distance from the Beloved never existed. What you seek is seeking you, and you are already on the same side of the wall.

sagesses.titre: Do Not Sleep, the Caravan Departs
sagesses.texte: Tonight, do not sleep: this is the hour when the caravan of lovers sets out.
sagesses.commentaire: There are moments when grace passes within reach, and the slumber of habit makes us miss them. The vigilance of the heart is not tension: it is staying awake to the quiet call of the Real. Do not let your daily drowsiness make you miss the departure.

sagesses.texte: Seek the balm in the very place where you received the wound.
sagesses.commentaire: We flee the place that made us suffer, though it often holds the key to our healing. What broke you also bears the teaching that mends you. Spiritual courage is to return to the site of the wound to find the oil there.

sagesses.texte: A merchant asks his caged parrot what message it would have him carry to its brethren still free in the Indies. Over there, a bird hears it and drops down stiff. On his return the merchant tells the tale; at once his own parrot falls likewise, and he throws it outside, thinking to mourn it. The bird flies off: its brother had taught it the one way out of the cage—to play dead to what one takes oneself to be.
sagesses.commentaire: Freedom is gained not by forcing the bars but by dying to the image the cage demands of us. As long as you believe yourself to be what your prison tells you, you remain in it. Die to that false self, and the door opens as if of its own accord.

sagesses.titre: The Gardener and the Three Thieves
sagesses.texte: Three men enter an orchard together to plunder it. The gardener, rather than confront them united, gently separates them, and one by one, each alone, sends them packing. As long as they held together, their strength overwhelmed him.
sagesses.commentaire: Our appetites in league govern us by their number and their confusion. The inner work consists in naming them one by one, in isolating them from the tumult. What is regarded separately loses the power it drew from the throng.

sagesses.texte: People feel an elephant in the dark. One touches its ear and says: a fan. Another its leg: a pillar. Another its trunk: a pipe. Each speaks truly of a part, and all of them quarrel. A single candle would have ended the dispute.
sagesses.commentaire: Our disputes about the Real arise less from falsehood than from the partiality of our touch. Each defends the portion he has grasped as though it were the whole. The light that reconciles is not one more opinion, but a vision that embraces all.

sagesses.texte: A jackal falls into a vat of dye and comes out gaudily coloured. Seeing himself so bedecked, he proclaims himself a peacock before the other beasts. But they say to him: then let us hear the peacock's cry. He could not, and his pretension betrayed him.
sagesses.commentaire: One may borrow the colours of a spiritual station without having its inner song. The appearances of holiness last only a while: sooner or later, trial demands the authentic cry. Be what you wear, or the dye will eventually fade.

sagesses.texte: Two reeds drink from the same water: one stays hollow, the other becomes sugar. All depends on what one does with what one receives.
sagesses.commentaire: The same grace waters all hearts, yet does not yield the same fruit in all. It is not the rain that is lacking, but the receptivity that differs. Ask yourself not what life pours into you, but into what you transform it.

sagesses.texte: Do not weep for what departs: perhaps it leaves so that something more beautiful may come.
sagesses.commentaire: Our hands clenched on what is slipping away can receive nothing. Loss often makes room; what we take for an impoverishment prepares a bounty. Loosen the grip, and watch which palm opens.

sagesses.texte: Beyond good and evil there is a field. I will wait for you there.
sagesses.commentaire: As long as we dwell in the tribunal of right and wrong, we remain divided by our judgements. There is a place of the soul prior to dispute, where at last we meet. True peace begins where we cease to be right against the other.

sagesses.texte: The birds set out in quest of the Sīmorgh, their king. After seven valleys of trial, only thirty survive. At the end, they find no king other than themselves: sī-morgh, thirty birds, are the Sīmorgh. The sought was the mirror of the seekers.
sagesses.commentaire: The long road toward God leads the purified soul back to its own depth, where the Real recognises itself in it as in a mirror. Not that God is you, but that your true being was never anything but a reflection of Him. The spiritual journey goes nowhere else: it goes to the most inward place.

sagesses.texte: To reach the Beloved, the bird of the soul crosses seven valleys: the Quest, Love, Knowledge, Detachment, Unity, Bewilderment, and at last Destitution, where one is lost in God.
sagesses.commentaire: The path is not a straight line but a series of ever more radical deaths to self. Each valley tears away a veil the previous one could not remove. At the end, it is not the possession of God but the extinction of all that was not Him.

sagesses.texte: The hoopoe summons the birds to the great journey. Each finds an excuse: the nightingale loves the rose too much, the parrot its gilded cage, the peacock its garden. To each refusal she answers that they mistake the image for the Beloved it reflects.
sagesses.commentaire: Our attachments are always loves of the reflection taken for the source. The danger is not to love the rose, but to stop at it. All that holds you back is a misread message: behind every beauty hides the invitation to ascend toward Beauty itself.

sagesses.texte: In the valley of bewilderment, the lover no longer knows whether he exists or not, whether he is within or without, drunk or sober; he knows only one thing: he loves.
sagesses.commentaire: At the approaches to the Real, the categories of reason dissolve and only the burning remains. This disarray is not a straying but an advance: one no longer understands because one is at last too close. Bewilderment is the science of the journey's end.

sagesses.texte: As long as you see another's fault as greater than your own, you have not yet set foot upon the path.
sagesses.commentaire: The eye turned toward a neighbour's faults is an eye that flees its own mirror. The sincere seeker becomes severe toward himself and lenient toward others. Reverse the gaze: the only fault over which you have power is your own.

sagesses.texte: Some moths wish to know what the flame is. One approaches it and returns to speak of its glow, another grazes it and tells of its heat. But a third hurls itself in and is consumed: it alone knew, and could report nothing.
sagesses.commentaire: Knowledge about God and the knowledge of God differ as the account differs from the blaze. As long as one returns to bear witness, one has merely observed. The ultimate truth cannot be reported: it is paid for with one's whole self.

sagesses.texte: A king passed a dervish in rags and looked him up and down. The dervish said to him: we are not so different; you serve what I trample underfoot, and what you fear, I hold as nothing.
sagesses.commentaire: True wealth lies not in possession but in liberation from need. The one poor in God is more sovereign than the king imprisoned by his goods. Ask yourself who serves whom, between you and what you hoard.

sagesses.texte: I sought the secret of the world from house to house; it was in mine, and I was not there.
sagesses.commentaire: We scour the horizons for what waits, patient, in our deserted interior. The absence is not God's but our own from ourselves. Come back to dwell in your own house: the Host has been there from the beginning.

sagesses.texte: This cup you hold already overflows with what you go begging for elsewhere.
sagesses.commentaire: The forgetful soul asks from outside what it carries in superabundance. The poverty we feel is an amnesia, not a real lack. Drink from your own cup: there you will find the wine you were about to beg for.

sagesses.texte: He who wants the rose must consent to the thorn; he who wants the treasure must face the serpent that guards it.
sagesses.commentaire: Every true beauty is guarded by a trial that turns away the slothful. Pain is not the enemy of joy; it is its sentinel. Do not ask for a path without brambles; ask for hands patient enough to pass through them.

sagesses.texte: I am drunk on a wine that no press has trodden, and my intoxication knows no morning after.
sagesses.commentaire: The intoxication of which the poet speaks is not a flight but a fullness: that of the soul made drunk on presence. This wine leaves neither debt nor hangover, for it comes from on high. Seek the ecstasy that makes you more lucid, not the one that ruins you.

sagesses.texte: If the mirror of your heart does not reflect the Beloved, it is because the dust of care has covered it.
sagesses.commentaire: The heart is by nature a mirror turned toward the Real; it is our cares that tarnish it. The spiritual way is less an acquisition than a polishing. Wipe away the dust of greed and fear, and the image will reappear of its own accord.

sagesses.texte: The children of Adam are the limbs of a single body, sprung from one same essence. Let one limb suffer, and the others can know no rest.
sagesses.commentaire: Humanity is not a sum of individuals but a single organism in which each is a limb. Indifference to another's pain is a disease of the whole body. As long as one part of mankind suffers while you sleep at ease, you have not earned the name of man.

sagesses.texte: A raindrop fell from the cloud and saw itself absurd before the immensity of the sea. The sea welcomed it, a shell gathered it up, and time made of it a pearl. Its humility raised it to the rank of a royal jewel.
sagesses.commentaire: The one who knows himself to be little draws the grace that transfigures him; the proud, by contrast, remain stagnant water. Consented lowliness is not a humiliation, but the matrix of nobility. Acknowledge your smallness, and you will become capable of the pearl.

sagesses.texte: Musk is that which reveals its scent of itself, not what the merchant boasts of.
sagesses.commentaire: Real virtue has no need to proclaim itself: it is breathed before it is spoken. The one who must announce his holiness already lacks it. Be musk, not advertisement: let your fragrance speak for you without your having to cry it aloud.

sagesses.texte: The fool, while he keeps silent, passes for wise; his tongue then unmasks him.
sagesses.commentaire: Speech is the trial of the inner self: it reveals what silence concealed. Better to hold one's tongue and leave doubt hanging than to open it and lift all uncertainty. Before you speak, ask yourself whether your words add anything to your silence.

sagesses.texte: A sage was reproached for not instructing a vile man. He answered: the rain of heaven, however beneficent, makes flowers grow in the garden and rank weeds in the marsh. The good falls equally; it is the soil that decides.
sagesses.commentaire: The same teaching nourishes one and poisons another, according to the ground that receives it. The master gives without measuring, but the fruit depends on the disciple. Work first to become good soil; then the least rain will be a blessing to you.

sagesses.titre: The Shared Bread of the Desert
sagesses.texte: Two travellers crossed the desert: one rich and stout, the other frugal and light. The provisions ran out. The glutton failed first; the abstemious one, used to little, survived and saved him. His temperance was his mount.
sagesses.commentaire: What we take for deprivation is often a training for the trial to come. The temperate soul crosses the famines that the sated soul cannot endure. Accustom yourself to little while you have abundance, so as to stand firm when all is wanting.

sagesses.texte: Whichever way I turn my eyes, it is always your face that appears to me.
sagesses.commentaire: To the purified eye, all the world's beauty is a fragmented reflection of a single Beauty. The faces we love are not rivals of God but His places of transparency. Learn to see through forms, and the manifold will lead you back to the One.

sagesses.texte: The wave that believes itself separate from the sea wears itself out running toward it, though it has never left it.
sagesses.commentaire: The feeling of separation from the Real is the root of all anguish, and it rests upon an illusion. You do not have to rejoin the ocean: you have only to stop believing yourself apart from it. The return is not a journey but an awakening.

sagesses.texte: Zuleikha had Joseph painted on every wall of her dwelling; wherever she looked, she saw him. Later, she learned to utter the divine Name in such a way that, in every word, it was the Beloved she named without naming.
sagesses.commentaire: Passionate love can be the apprenticeship of a higher love, if it consents to be surpassed. What begins with the idol may end in true adoration, when the heart at last deciphers who was hidden behind the face. Every sincere longing is on its way toward its source.

sagesses.texte: Beauty could not bear to remain hidden: it showed itself in the mirror of creatures to behold itself there and to be loved.
sagesses.commentaire: The world is not an obstacle between God and us, but the mirror in which His Beauty gives itself to be seen. Creation is an act of love that wants to be recognised. Each beautiful thing is a discreet invitation to ascend toward the One reflected in it.

sagesses.titre: The Elephant and the Dark Room
sagesses.texte: Blind men were led into a dark room where an elephant had been placed. Each felt a part of it and gave an opposite description. All were speaking of the same beast, but none had it whole.
sagesses.commentaire: This image, which Sanāʾī made known before Rūmī, shows that our theologies are partial gropings at the same Real. The quarrel is born of taking one's portion for the totality. The humility of the mind is to know that one is feeling where others feel elsewhere.

sagesses.texte: Truth is a walled garden: one enters it not by the gate of reasoning, but by that of self-annihilation.
sagesses.commentaire: Logic leads as far as the threshold, then stops, for the Real is not a conclusion but a presence. The self that wants to understand is precisely what blocks the gate. Where thought capitulates, the stripped-bare soul passes through.

sagesses.texte: The human being is a guest house. Each morning a new arrival: a joy, a sorrow, a meanness. Welcome them all, even the crowd of griefs, for each may have been sent to empty you and make you room.
sagesses.commentaire: We would like to sort our inner visitors, keeping the joys and chasing away the sorrows; but this is to do them violence and to close ourselves to their message. Each state passed through without flinching hollows out a new space in us. Welcome even mourning: it clears the house for a greater Host.

sagesses.texte: To light a candle in full sunlight—such is what he does who seeks outside the light he carries.
sagesses.commentaire: The soul wears itself out in borrowed practices and distant quests, ignoring the hearth that burns within it. The whole art consists in turning back toward what already gives light. Grace is not absent: it is our gaze that is turned the wrong way.

sagesses.texte: Only true gold is tried by fire and touchstone; no one troubles over a worthless coin.
sagesses.commentaire: The hardest trials often come to those who carry the most worth within them. Suffering is not a punishment but an assay that reveals your mettle. If life puts you in the crucible, it is because it presumes you to be gold.

sagesses.titre: Lift Your Head from the Manger
sagesses.texte: As long as the donkey keeps its head in the manger, it will never raise its eyes toward the rider.
sagesses.commentaire: Absorption in consumption and comfort keeps the soul bent down, unable to see what surpasses it. The mere act of lifting the head is already a spiritual act. Turn for a moment from the trough, and you will discover that you are being called.

sagesses.texte: Solomon knew the language of the birds; yet many men do not even know how to speak to their own hearts.
sagesses.commentaire: We dream of wonders and distant powers, while remaining deaf to the nearest voice, our own. The first language to learn is that of one's own interior. Before you would understand the heavens, listen to what speaks in your breast.

sagesses.texte: When the seed is buried, it believes itself lost in the dark; it does not know that it has been planted.
sagesses.commentaire: The dark nights of the soul resemble a burial, and one believes oneself forgotten there. But what seems a fall is sometimes a fertile sowing. Be patient in the darkness: it is not a tomb, it is a furrow.

sagesses.texte: Love is the astrolabe of the mysteries of God.
sagesses.commentaire: As the astrolabe reads the sky without touching it, love measures the divine heights that the intellect cannot reach. One knows the Real only by loving it, for love is the organ of a higher vision. To love is already to begin to know.

sagesses.titre: The Cry That Calls Forth the Answer
sagesses.texte: It is the cry of the hungry infant that draws the milk up to the mother's breast. Cry out your need, then, and grace will flow.
sagesses.commentaire: Prayer is not a formula but a call wrenched from want. The more your thirst is confessed, the more it draws what quenches it. Be not ashamed of your poverty before God: it is the very magnet of His generosity.

sagesses.texte: There is a knowledge that comes from without, through books and masters; and a knowledge that wells up from within, like water from the spring. The first runs dry, the second never.
sagesses.commentaire: Accumulated learning remains external until it has sprung up as intimate knowledge. One may retain everything and know nothing, or forget everything and taste the essential. Seek less to fill your head than to dig down to your spring.

sagesses.texte: Without the rain of the cloud's tears, how would the garden smile?
sagesses.commentaire: The soul's tears are not a sterile misfortune but the water that makes the inner self bloom. Something in us must pour out for joy to grow. Do not refuse your tears: they water a garden you cannot yet see.

sagesses.texte: The drum gives off a sound only because it is hollow within. Full, it would remain mute.
sagesses.commentaire: It is our inner emptiness, the stripping away of the self, that lets the voice of the Spirit resound in us. As long as we are full of ourselves, nothing else finds an echo there. Learn to empty yourself so as to become an instrument.

sagesses.titre: The Path Is Made by Walking
sagesses.texte: Set foot upon the way, and the way will appear.
sagesses.commentaire: We would like to see the whole path before setting out, but the spiritual trail reveals itself only to the one who walks. Clarity comes with commitment, not before it. Take the first step into the darkness; the ground will reveal itself beneath your sole.

sagesses.texte: Do not judge the depth of the water by the straw that floats on its surface.
sagesses.commentaire: The visible eddies of a life do not tell its true measure; what stirs the surface is not what flows below. Do not trust superficial signs to judge a being or yourself. Beneath the chatter of appearances, a graver water moves in silence.

sagesses.titre: The Fire and Water of Rābiʿa
sagesses.texte: Rābiʿa was seen running, a torch in one hand and a pitcher of water in the other. Why? To burn paradise and quench hell, she said, so that God might be worshipped for His own sake alone, and not out of hope or fear.
sagesses.commentaire: Pure love does not bargain: it does not serve God for a wage nor out of dread of punishment. As long as reward or threat moves us, it is still ourselves we are seeking. Worship the Beloved for His Face, and you will be free at last.

sagesses.texte: A man planted himself before the palace and demanded his share of the kingdom. They took him for mad. He answered: this king inherited the throne from another, who held it from yet another; it is only an inn where each one passes through. Why should I not have my turn to lodge in it?
sagesses.commentaire: Power and possession are but a wayside lodging that death keeps passing from hand to hand. To cling to what merely passes between our fingers—there is the true madness. Inhabit the world as a traveller, not as an owner.

sagesses.texte: A fly fell into a jar of honey; sated, it wished to fly away, but its wings were caught. Before dying glued fast, it moaned: a single grain of this honey would have been enough for me.
sagesses.commentaire: The world's sweetnesses are wholesome in just measure and deadly in excess. It is not pleasure that ruins us, but the unbridled engulfing. Taste the honey, but keep your wings free so as to be able to leave again.

sagesses.texte: Do not despair: it is in the darkest night that the dawn is already on its way.
sagesses.commentaire: Discouragement believes the darkness to be final, when it is the moment that precedes the turning. The law of the heart wills that the light rise from where it was least expected. Hold fast in the dark: it is not the end, it is the eve of day.

sagesses.texte: A gentle word given in kindness is worth more than alms followed by an offence.
sagesses.commentaire: A material gift given without regard humiliates, whereas a kindly word lifts up at no cost. True generosity lies first in the manner, not in the amount. Before you give of your goods, give of your courtesy: it never runs dry.

sagesses.texte: A king envied the peaceful sleep of a poor cobbler. The sage said to him: you wear the crown, but you also bear the weight of a kingdom; he has nothing but his tools, yet he sleeps without a care. The burden grows with what one possesses.
sagesses.commentaire: What we hold holds us in turn: each possession added is one more worry to watch over. The soul's peace lies not in having but in detachment. Ask yourself whether you possess your goods, or whether it is they who possess you.

sagesses.texte: Whoever plants a walnut tree knows he will not eat its fruit himself; he labours for mouths he will never see.
sagesses.commentaire: The highest generosity works for a harvest it will never enjoy. To sow without expecting to reap, that is the selfless gesture par excellence. Plant trees whose shade and fruit others will share: this is how one prolongs the good beyond oneself.

sagesses.texte: Whoever wants the pearl does not stay upon the shore: he must dive to the very bottom, where the perils lie in wait.
sagesses.commentaire: The treasures of the soul are not gathered at the surface; they demand a descent into our dreaded depths. The safety of the bank never yields the pearl. Consent to dive where you are afraid: that is where what you seek lies resting.

sagesses.texte: The sun never ceases to shine when the cloud conceals it; it is your sight that darkens, not its light.
sagesses.commentaire: The divine presence knows no eclipse; it is our states that veil it from time to time. Do not mistake a felt absence for a real one. When you no longer feel the light, know that it still shines above your clouds.

sagesses.texte: The grain complains beneath the millstone; but without being ground, how would it ever become bread to nourish others?
sagesses.commentaire: The trials that crush us are sometimes the grinding that makes us useful and nourishing to others. To remain an intact grain is to remain barren. Consent to be ground: it is the price of becoming bread that can be shared.

sagesses.texte: When a door closes, do not stand before it moaning: know that a key has been placed within you.
sagesses.commentaire: Obstacles never come without an inner resource accompanying them. Dismay fixes us upon the lock and makes us forget the key we carry. Search within before lamenting without: the way out is often hidden there.

sagesses.texte: Open a window between two hearts, and you will need neither wall nor door.
sagesses.commentaire: True communion does not come through explanations or justifications, but through a transparency of soul to soul. Where hearts open, defences become useless. Seek less to convince the other than to pierce a window towards them.

sagesses.texte: The friend's gaze is the alchemy that turns the copper of your being into gold.
sagesses.commentaire: To be truly seen by one who loves us transforms us more than all our efforts. The spiritual companion does not correct us; he reveals the gold we bore beneath our copper. Choose with care the eyes under which you stand: they shape you.

sagesses.texte: Do not content yourself with being the cup: be the wine.
sagesses.commentaire: One may spend a lifetime holding the form of religion without ever tasting its intoxication. The cup is precious, but it is made only for the wine. Do not cling so to the vessel that you forget the liquor it is meant to carry.

sagesses.texte: You seek the enemy without, while within you feed the brigand who plunders you.
sagesses.commentaire: Our worst adversaries are not those we accuse but the passions we harbour. The spiritual struggle is waged first against oneself. Before closing your door to others, flush out the robber you lodge within your own house.

sagesses.texte: A candle has nothing to lose by lighting a thousand other candles; its flame remains whole.
sagesses.commentaire: Spiritual good is not divided by being shared: it multiplies. To give one's light does not impoverish, unlike the goods of the world. Do not be afraid to pass on what you have received: your flame will be no less for it, but the world will be brighter.

sagesses.texte: The green almond is bitter, but let it ripen: time will turn its bitterness into sweetness.
sagesses.commentaire: Many fruits of the soul are harsh at first and grow sweet only with patience. To want to pluck everything before its time is to condemn oneself to bitterness. Give things, and yourself, the time to ripen; sweetness will come in its hour.

sagesses.texte: There is a prayer that has neither words nor forms, where the lover and the Beloved breathe with a single breath.
sagesses.commentaire: Rites and formulas are boats; out on the open sea, the boat itself fades away. The highest prayer is no longer a request but a shared presence. When you have nothing left to say and yet you remain, perhaps you are at last truly praying.

sagesses.titre: The Physician Who Reads the Pulse
sagesses.texte: A wise physician, to uncover the secret ailment of a sick woman, took her pulse and had her recite a thousand names; at one alone, her blood stirred. He knew then where her hidden wound lay.
sagesses.commentaire: The soul betrays its deepest attachment through faint tremors that only an attentive gaze can detect. We do not always know where we are bound; the true master reads it within us. Learn to watch within yourself for the name that quickens your blood: it tells you where you truly are.

sagesses.texte: When the light enters through the window, one sees even the specks of dust dancing in the beam.
sagesses.commentaire: Beneath the divine gaze, the humblest beings reveal a secret movement of joy and adoration. Nothing is inert in the illumined creation; all things dance, in their own way, towards their source. Open a window in your heart, and you will see even your dust dance.

sagesses.texte: It is the pearls that we admire, but it is the hidden thread that holds them together as a necklace.
sagesses.commentaire: The dazzling multiplicity of the world rests upon a discreet Unity that the ordinary eye does not see. The visible hangs all its beauty upon an invisible that binds it. Seek, behind the scattering of the pearls, the single thread that orders them.

sagesses.texte: To ask why one loves is to have not yet loved: true love has no why.
sagesses.commentaire: Love that explains itself by reasons dies when the reasons change. The love of God, however, hangs upon no condition: it loves because it loves. Seek within yourself this gratuitous love, without calculation or motive, for it alone never betrays.

sagesses.texte: Reason leads you to the threshold of the king, but it is love that brings you into the chamber.
sagesses.commentaire: The intellect is a good servant for drawing near the mystery, but it does not take the final step. Beyond a certain point, love alone has the right of entry. Honour your reason, but know at which threshold to leave it in order to go further.

sagesses.texte: You seek at the world's end the One who is nearer to you than your own vein.
sagesses.commentaire: The distance we set between ourselves and the Real is entirely of our own making. He is not far off: it is we who have strayed from our centre. Cease running: return to that intimate point where He awaits you, nearer than your breath.

sagesses.texte: Three fish lived in a pond threatened by fishermen. The wise one set off at once for the river. The cunning one played dead in the net and escaped at the right moment. The heedless one, who kept putting off his flight, ended up in the net.
sagesses.commentaire: Faced with spiritual danger, there is the one who acts ahead of time, the one who outwits it at the right moment, and the one whom procrastination destroys. Wisdom does not wait for the eleventh hour. Do not put off until tomorrow the departure your heart knows is needed today.

sagesses.titre: The Lamp From Hand to Hand
sagesses.texte: Truth is a lamp passed from hand to hand; each must light it from his own, for none can carry it in his place.
sagesses.commentaire: One receives the transmission, but no one can perform the inner kindling in our place. The master holds out the flame; the disciple must offer his own wick. Do not content yourself with admiring another's lamp: bring yours near and let it catch fire.

sagesses.texte: He who sees only the ink upon the page has not yet read: the meaning dwells beyond the strokes.
sagesses.commentaire: One may know every letter of a sacred text and miss its spirit. The form is only a vehicle for a meaning that passes through it. Learn to read beyond the letter, there where the ink fades before what it meant to say.

sagesses.texte: Intoxication passes; sobriety endures. And the station of the one who returns to himself in the presence of God is worth more than the ecstasy of the one who loses himself in it.
sagesses.commentaire: The beginner dreams of rapture, of tears and flashes of light. But spiritual maturity is not measured by the intensity of emotion: it is measured by the presence that remains when the emotion has fallen silent. Return to yourself, lucid, and stand upright in the ordinary: that is the highest.

sagesses.texte: Oneness is that the created be set apart from the Eternal as the Eternal is set apart from the created.
sagesses.commentaire: To profess the One is not a formula but an operation upon oneself: to cease confusing my will with His, my wanting with the real. Where I withdraw, He appears. Today, in a small decision, try to distinguish what springs from your greed from what comes as a gift.

sagesses.texte: Sufism is that God make you die to yourself in order to make you live by Him.
sagesses.commentaire: To die to oneself is not to destroy oneself: it is to cease holding the centre one has never occupied. What dies is the illusion of being the author of one's own life. What is born is an existence received with each breath. Breathe today knowing that this breath is lent to you.

sagesses.texte: Address people with speech to the measure of their understanding, not to the measure of yours.
sagesses.commentaire: The love of truth can disguise itself as violence when it ignores the one who listens. Wisdom is not to say all that one knows, but to give what the other can receive. Today, before correcting someone, ask yourself what share they can bear without breaking.

sagesses.texte: Glory be to me! How immense is my dignity!
sagesses.commentaire: This scandalous utterance is no raving of pride: it is the moment when the "I" has so thoroughly vanished that it is no longer the man who speaks. When the drop falls silent, it is the ocean one hears within it. Beware, however, of mimicking it: true humility does not proclaim itself, it is lived in silence.

sagesses.titre: To Slough Off the Self Like a Snake
sagesses.texte: I shed myself as the snake sheds its skin; then I looked: and the self, it was He.
sagesses.commentaire: Every inner path is a moulting: one leaves behind a skin grown too narrow, not without tearing. What you take for your identity is often only an old casing. Today, single out a certainty about yourself that you might shed, as one casts off a garment grown too tight.

sagesses.texte: At the first House, I saw the House; at the second, I saw the Master of the House; at the third, I saw neither House nor Master.
sagesses.commentaire: One begins by cherishing forms, holy places, rites; then one seeks the One they point to; at last one effaces oneself to the point that nothing remains but He. The stages are not disowned: each prepares the next. Honour today the form you practise, without forgetting towards Whom it points.

sagesses.texte: For thirty years I sought God; when I turned my gaze, I saw that it was He who was seeking me.
sagesses.commentaire: We believe we are leading the quest, when we are the ones being sought. The desire that drives you towards the Source is already the Source drawing you to it. This evening, turn the prayer around: before asking to be found, feel how greatly you are already awaited.

sagesses.texte: I love You with two loves: a love of self-interest, and a love worthy of You alone. The love of self-interest keeps me occupied with You to the exclusion of all else. As for the love worthy of You, it is You who lift the veils that I may see You.
sagesses.commentaire: Rābiʿa does not scorn the love that asks: she acknowledges it, but knows it is only a threshold. Beyond the benefit there is the Benefactor; beyond the gift, the Giver. Today, give thanks for a grace received, then go further: love the hand that gives more than what it sets down.

sagesses.texte: My heart is so filled with my Beloved that there remains no room in it to love or to hate another.
sagesses.commentaire: When a presence truly fills the heart, petty grudges and attachments lose their grip for want of room. Inner peace comes not from fighting the passions one by one, but from letting grow what makes them trifling. What do you wish, today, to let grow within you until the rest fades away?

sagesses.texte: O my God, give Your enemies what You will of the goods of this world, and Your friends the other world; for as for me, it is enough to be occupied with You.
sagesses.commentaire: The purest love bargains for nothing, not even paradise. It has found its rest not in the reward but in the relationship itself. This gratuitousness is not reserved for the saints: each time you give without expecting anything, you drink a drop of it. Today, offer a gesture for which no one will thank you.

sagesses.texte: My God, the stars are shining, the eyes of men have closed, the kings have bolted their doors; and every lover is alone with his beloved. And here am I, alone with You.
sagesses.commentaire: The night strips the world of its tumult and gives the soul back to its secret. What the day scatters, the darkness gathers. You need not keep vigil until dawn: seek, within your day, a single moment as bare, where nothing any longer stands between you and the Essential.

sagesses.texte: The sign of the knower is that he unites with all without mingling with anyone, and that he is alone even in the midst of the crowd.
sagesses.commentaire: True inwardness does not flee from men: it passes through the crowd without dissolving in it. One can be fully present to others while keeping, deep within, a chamber that none enters but He. Today, be there for those you meet, but do not lose the silent thread that binds you within.

sagesses.texte: The more a man knows God, the more he is lost in Him, and the more humble and near he becomes.
sagesses.commentaire: True knowledge does not swell: it prostrates. He who knows little thinks himself great; he who knows much measures the abyss and bows his head. If your knowledge makes you hard or superior, mistrust it: this is not yet knowledge, but its flattering shadow.

sagesses.texte: The repentance of the common folk is to turn away from sins; the repentance of the elect is to turn away from forgetfulness.
sagesses.commentaire: We think it enough to avoid manifest evil. But the subtlest veil is not the fault: it is distraction, the forgetting of the Presence at the heart of the most ordinary things. Today, your true repentance may be not to cease an act, but to return from a forgetting.

sagesses.texte: I am the Truth.
sagesses.commentaire: Three words that cost him his life. It was not to take himself for God, but to bear witness that within him nothing remained but He. When iron glows red in the fire, it says "I am fire" without ceasing to be iron. Keep this mystery with modesty: what is paid for at the price of blood is not repeated lightly.

sagesses.titre: The Two Units of Love's Prayer
sagesses.texte: Love is two bowings of prayer whose ablution is made only with blood.
sagesses.commentaire: To love truly costs; it is not the sweet feeling one imagines, but a stripping bare that may bleed. Everything precious is purified in an ordeal. Where you love to the point that it costs you without your complaining, you touch upon something of the truth of Ḥallāj.

sagesses.texte: Between You and me there is an "I am" that torments me; remove, by Your "I am," my "I am" from between us two.
sagesses.commentaire: The only obstacle between the soul and its Source is the little self that believes itself the owner of itself. As long as it lays claim, it forms a screen. The highest prayer is not to ask for goods, but to ask to be effaced. This evening, dare to desire not to have more, but to be a little less cluttered with yourself.

sagesses.texte: He said to me: the letter is a veil, and the veil is a veil.
sagesses.commentaire: Even the most sacred words can become screens if one halts at them. The map is not the country; the finger pointing at the moon is not the moon. Love your books, but do not make them your dwelling. Today, after reading a beautiful saying, close the book and remain, for a moment, in that towards which it points.

sagesses.texte: He said to me: you will not know Me through yourself, but you will know yourself through Me.
sagesses.commentaire: We believe we set out from ourselves to reach the absolute, but it is the reverse: it is in the light of the Origin that our own face becomes legible. One cannot light oneself. Today, cease for a moment the anxious introspection and let yourself be looked upon by one greater than you.

sagesses.texte: He said to me: halt within knowledge, and do not pass beyond knowledge, for you would lose yourself.
sagesses.commentaire: There is a time to advance and a time to keep still. Spiritual haste is a disguised greed. To know how to halt where one is, to dwell fully in one's station before desiring another, is already a form of maturity. Where, today, are you hurrying in vain?

sagesses.titre: The Sign of Relying on One's Works
sagesses.texte: One of the signs that one relies on one's own works is the loss of hope when a fault occurs.
sagesses.commentaire: If a fall crushes you to the point of despair, it is because you were secretly counting on your virtue, not on mercy. The sinner who rises again by trusting is worth more than the devout one who takes pride in his uprightness. When you stumble today, rise again without dramatising: hope does not rest upon you.

sagesses.texte: Bury your existence in the earth of obscurity; for what springs from what has not been buried bears no perfected fruit.
sagesses.commentaire: The seed bears fruit only when buried, invisible, forgetful of itself. Likewise, what you do seeking to be seen remains barren. The most fruitful good is the one you accomplish in the shadows, without counting it. Today, perform a deed that no one will know of, and let it germinate in secret.

sagesses.texte: What you seek for His sake does not escape you; what you seek for yourself never suffices you.
sagesses.commentaire: Greed has a strange property: the more it obtains, the hungrier it grows. Desire turned towards the Source, however, is sated with little, for it covets not things but the One who gives them. Observe one of your cravings today: do you seek it to fill a void, or to serve one greater than yourself?

sagesses.texte: Nothing draws you so near to Him as the sense of your destitution; nothing draws you so far from Him as the sense of your self-sufficiency.
sagesses.commentaire: The one rich in heart believes he needs nothing: he has closed his door. The poor one holds out his hands, and it is through that opening that grace enters. Your acknowledged weakness is a window, your supposed strength a wall. Today, where you feel destitute, do not hide: it is your point of passage.

sagesses.texte: Do not carry into your today the worry of your tomorrows: each day has enough in what it holds.
sagesses.commentaire: Worry anticipates burdens that the present moment does not yet ask us to bear. To live is to answer what is here, not what might be. Grace is given drop by drop in the present, never in advance. Today, set down the weight of tomorrow: it does not yet belong to you.

sagesses.texte: The lights of hearts and of inner eyes shine only by the light of heaven; turn your mirror, then, towards it.
sagesses.commentaire: The heart does not produce its own brightness: it receives it, as the moon receives the sun. Your whole task is to turn the mirror, not to manufacture the light. Today, rather than forcing an illumination, simply dispose yourself: clean, turn, wait. The rest comes from elsewhere.

sagesses.titre: The Gift According to His Knowledge
sagesses.texte: It may be that He gives to you by withholding, and that He withholds from you by giving.
sagesses.commentaire: Some lack that grieves you is sometimes your greatest protection; some good obtained, your secret loss. We judge gifts by their husk, without seeing their kernel. Before a deprivation today, suspend your verdict: perhaps you are being spared, in the shadows, an ill you do not see.

sagesses.titre: The Gratitude That Binds the Blessing
sagesses.texte: Whoever does not give thanks for graces exposes himself to losing them; whoever gives thanks for them binds them by their fetters.
sagesses.commentaire: Gratitude is not a courtesy: it is what holds the blessing, as a mooring holds the boat. The ungrateful lets slip what he has, because he no longer even sees it. Today, name three ordinary graces you never notice: by naming them, you hold them fast.

sagesses.texte: O God, none has known You, and yet none has known You except through You.
sagesses.commentaire: The knowledge of the Absolute is a living paradox: one cannot grasp Him, and one can grasp nothing without Him. Our intelligence misses Him, and it is by His light that it sees. Hold these two truths together: be humble in what you believe you know, confident in what is given you to understand.

sagesses.texte: O God, I bring neither devotion nor works; I come to Your door with empty hands, a beggar for Your grace alone.
sagesses.commentaire: Before the infinite, our merits are specks of dust. To come with empty hands is not a failure but the condition of every true gift: one fills only what is hollow. Today, dare to draw near to an encounter, a prayer, with nothing to display, simply available and bare.

sagesses.texte: With each breath that enters, life is prolonged; with each breath that leaves, the heart rejoices. Thus each breath bears two graces, and each grace calls for a gratitude.
sagesses.commentaire: The most ordinary breathing is a twofold marvel: to receive and to give back, to welcome and to let go. We do it thousands of times without thinking. Today, take three conscious breaths: feel, in this silent exchange, the image of the whole spiritual life, made of welcome and surrender.

sagesses.texte: O God, the love You have sown in my heart, let it not wither; and the lamp You have kindled there, let it not go out.
sagesses.commentaire: The desire for the Essential is not of our own making: it has been placed in us. Our task is only not to smother it beneath worries and distractions. Watch this evening over that little flame: what have you, of late, let grow cold within you, and how might you revive it with a simple gesture?

sagesses.texte: The beginning of trusting surrender is to be in the hands of God as the dead body is in the hands of the one who washes it.
sagesses.commentaire: The dead body does not resist, does not argue, does not demand: it lets itself be turned. This surrender is no lazy passivity but a deep relaxation, the end of the self's tension. Where today you fight against what is, try, for a moment, to let yourself be turned without stiffening.

sagesses.texte: He who frets over his sustenance frets over what is already guaranteed to him, and neglects what is asked of him.
sagesses.commentaire: The anguish of tomorrow devours the energy of today. What is necessary, says wisdom, follows you as the shadow follows the body; what truly depends on you is your present uprightness. Today, shift one of your material worries towards the one useful question: what is, now, the right thing to do?

sagesses.texte: The heart is the house of God; woe to him who lets another than Him enter it.
sagesses.commentaire: Our inwardness has a rightful guest; when we crowd it with idols, obsessions, images, we contest the dwelling with Him. To clean the house of the heart is to dismiss the false occupants. What invasive tenant, a grudge, a covetousness, a fear, squats today in the room that is not its own?

sagesses.texte: Know that the key to the knowledge of God is the knowledge of your soul.
sagesses.commentaire: We would like to climb to heaven without descending into ourselves. But the path towards the Source passes through the narrow door of the self: it is by examining ourselves that we learn what it is to depend, to receive, to lack. Today, grant yourself a quarter of an hour of honest examination: what did I truly want, this morning, behind my acts?

sagesses.texte: I probed every doctrine, until I understood that at the end of knowing one must taste and no longer merely understand.
sagesses.commentaire: One may read everything about honey without knowing its flavour. The knowledge that transforms is not the kind one accumulates but the kind one experiences. There comes a moment when one must close the books and undergo the experience. What truth, already read a thousand times, awaits you today to be at last lived?

sagesses.texte: The soul is like a rider: if you loosen the reins upon your mount, it hurls you down; if you hold them with gentleness and firmness, it carries you to the goal.
sagesses.commentaire: Our inclinations are not enemies to be killed but a force to be schooled. Neither flatter them nor break them: lead them. True discipline is neither laxity nor violence, but a hand both firm and kind. Over which inclination, today, can you gently take up the reins again, without inner war?

sagesses.texte: Examine your soul at every moment; call it to account for its intentions before calling it to account for its deeds.
sagesses.commentaire: Muḥāsibī, "the one who keeps his accounts", teaches us that an act is worth what its hidden intention is worth. One can do good for bad reasons and so deceive oneself. Inner vigilance is no morbid scruple but a loving lucidity. Today, before a good deed, ask gently: for whom, exactly?

sagesses.texte: The finest of works is to watch over the thoughts of the heart, for from there all the rest proceeds.
sagesses.commentaire: Before the act there is the thought; before the word, the secret leaning. Whoever watches the spring watches the whole river. We cannot lastingly mend our conduct without observing what gives rise to it within. Today, catch a thought at its birth, and choose whether to feed it or let it pass.

sagesses.texte: Sufism is this: what you hold in your head, abandon it; what you grasp in your hand, give it away; and what befalls you, do not flee from it.
sagesses.commentaire: Three gestures for a whole life: to lighten the mind of ready-made ideas, to open the clutching hand, to welcome the real as it comes. None requires a monastery; all are practised at table, at work, in the street. Which of the three, today, is hardest for you, and therefore most needful?

sagesses.titre: The Distance of a Single Step
sagesses.texte: He was asked what distance separates man from God. He answered: "A single step — the one that takes you out of yourself."
sagesses.commentaire: We imagine the Essential infinitely far off, at the end of immense labours. But the only distance is the thickness of our own self, and it is enough to step out of it. That step is taken not by force but by letting go. Where, today, do you cling so to yourself that you block His passage?

sagesses.texte: He who rejoices for others as for himself, and who bears in his heart no grudge against anyone — that one is free.
sagesses.commentaire: Envy and rancour are chains we forge for ourselves: they bind the jailer as much as the prisoner. To rejoice in another's good widens the heart; to envy it narrows it. Today, on learning of another's success, watch your first stirring, and choose joy rather than the pinch of resentment.

sagesses.texte: The greatest of men is not the one who works miracles, but the one whose heart is more tender than a mother's.
sagesses.commentaire: We admire wonders and overlook gentleness; yet nothing is rarer or higher than a heart truly tender. Holiness is measured not by powers but by the quality of presence. Today, gauge your greatness not by what you achieve, but by the tenderness you are able to show a weak one.

sagesses.texte: Whoever enters my house, give him to eat and do not question him about his faith: for he who is worthy of life with the Real is surely worthy of bread with me.
sagesses.commentaire: True hospitality does not sort guests by their beliefs: it recognises in every living being a guest of the Source. Feed first, judge afterwards — or rather, do not judge at all. Today, welcome someone without setting conditions on your kindness; give before knowing whether he "deserves" it.

sagesses.texte: What the poor in God taste cannot be acquired through books: it is acquired through hunger, wakefulness and service.
sagesses.commentaire: Certain kinds of knowledge are not read: they are lived in willing deprivation, in vigil, in self-giving. The knowledge of the heart has trial and service for its university. Today, rather than learning one more thing, serve someone concretely: that too is a form of knowing.

sagesses.texte: Keep company only with one who draws you towards God by his state before his words, and the sight of whom reminds you of the beyond.
sagesses.commentaire: There are presences that lift and others that weigh down, without even a word. In time we come to resemble those we keep company with. To choose one's companions is already to choose who one will become. Who, around you, makes you better by their presence alone? Draw nearer to them today.

sagesses.texte: The Sufi is called Sufi because of a purity ( ṣafāʾ ) that is in his heart; otherwise the name would be no more than a label.
sagesses.commentaire: One can wear the cloak without having the heart, and the title without the reality. What makes the mystic is neither appearance nor vocabulary but an inner limpidity. Today, beware the labels you bear — "believer", "sage", "good" — and ask whether the thing truly dwells within the word.

sagesses.texte: I was asked: "What is the remembrance of God?" I said: "It is to forget the remembrance within the Remembered."
sagesses.commentaire: The summit of prayer is not to see oneself praying, but to lose oneself in the One whom one prays to. As long as you watch yourself remembering, you still remember yourself. Today, in a moment of recollection, release control of your own devotion: let yourself be absorbed without watching yourself do it.

sagesses.texte: Prince of Balkh, he was hunting when a voice rose from his own saddle: "Is it not for this that you were created?" He dismounted, left the throne, and went forth, stripped bare, towards poverty.
sagesses.commentaire: The call does not always come from a sermon or a book: sometimes it bursts out in the midst of a hunt, a meeting, a meal. What turned Ibrāhīm around was a simple question about meaning. Today, dare to ask yourself, in the heart of some ordinary activity: is it truly for this that I exist?

sagesses.texte: We live in such sweetness that, did kings but know it, they would contend with us for it at the point of the sword.
sagesses.commentaire: Voluntary stripping is not deprivation but lightening: we have less, so we fear losing less; we own little, so nothing owns us. No palace can buy such freedom. Today, single out one object or one worry you could set down so as to breathe more freely.

sagesses.texte: You will not be among the righteous until you have crossed six thresholds: shut the door of ease and open that of effort; shut the door of pride and open that of humility.
sagesses.commentaire: Inner transformation speaks in doors to be shut and doors to be opened: none is far off, all are within our reach. One does not become righteous by dreaming, but by crossing a concrete threshold. Which door, today, are you asked to shut, and which to open in its place?

sagesses.texte: A disciple complained: "My master teaches me almost nothing." An elder answered: "That is because he teaches you the essential: his presence is the lesson, and his silence the commentary."
sagesses.commentaire: We expect words from a master, recipes, secrets; he offers first of all a way of being. The deepest transmission passes not through words but through the contagion of a state. Today, watch someone you admire not for what they say, but for the way they inhabit the moment.

sagesses.texte: A scholar came to a sheikh, brimming with questions and theories. The sheikh poured the tea without stopping; the cup overflowed. "You are this cup," he said: "how can I pour anything into you?"
sagesses.commentaire: We can receive only in the measure that we have made room. A mind too full of its own certainties is impervious to any fresh light. Before seeking to learn, one must consent to empty. What cumbersome conviction could you, today, set aside to make a little room?

sagesses.texte: An elder used to say: "The bird of the soul has two wings: fear and hope. With one alone, it falls; with both, it rises."
sagesses.commentaire: Too much fear crushes, too much hope lulls to sleep. The inner life rests in this living balance: to take one's frailty seriously without despairing, to hope for grace without presuming on oneself. Today, see which of your two wings is faltering — lucidity or trust — and give it strength again.

sagesses.texte: "How does God polish the soul?" asked a novice. The master pointed to a smooth stone at the bottom of the torrent: "See: not the hammer nor the hand, but the patient passing of the water, year after year."
sagesses.commentaire: We should like to be transformed all at once, by some thunderbolt revelation. But the most lasting work is done by gentle wearing: trials repeated, the frictions of daily life, the patience of the days. Today, do not despise the slow erosion of your rough edges: it is thus that one becomes smooth.

sagesses.texte: A sheikh held a candle in broad daylight. "Why?" he was asked. "To remind myself," he answered, "that all my light is nothing beneath the light from on high — and that I must keep it burning all the same."
sagesses.commentaire: Our little flame seems paltry before the immense brightness; that is no reason to put it out. Each must hold his own glimmer, however modest, within the great day that surpasses it. Today, do your small good without despising it because it is small: it has its place in the light.

sagesses.texte: "Why is the almond bitter in its shell?" a master would ask. "So that none may devour it before it has ripened, and the sweet fruit be kept for the one who knows how to wait."
sagesses.commentaire: The bitterness of a trial sometimes guards a fruit that is not yet ready. What repels you today may be defending a sweetness still to come. Patience is not resignation: it is the trust that time ripens what haste would spoil. What are you waiting to devour too soon?

sagesses.texte: An elder began each day with three questions: "Whose guest am I? Towards whom am I going? What has been entrusted to me for today?"
sagesses.commentaire: These three questions place our existence: received from a Source, oriented towards an end, charged with a task. To ask them is to leave off wandering and recover an axis. You need no definitive answers: the question alone already reorients the heart. Ask yourself, this morning, the third: what has been entrusted to you for today?

sagesses.texte: A man boasted of all he had accomplished. A sage said to him: "Count rather what you have received: you will see that you are not a creditor, but a debtor amply provided for."
sagesses.commentaire: Pride is born of a forgetting: of all that was given to us unearned — breath, sight, intelligence, encounters. To reverse the account is to pass from pride to gratitude. Today, instead of listing what you have done, enumerate what you have received: the scales will tip the other way.

sagesses.texte: "Master, I cannot see the end of the road." "The lantern," said the sheikh, "lights only the next step; and that is enough to walk the whole night through."
sagesses.commentaire: We should like to see the whole journey before setting out; but the inner light is never given save to the measure of the present step. To walk is to trust that the next lighting will come when it must. Today, stop demanding to see the end: attend only to the step you can take now.

sagesses.texte: A fish was asking everywhere where to find the ocean. An old turtle said to it: "Stop searching: that which bathes you and bears you up is the very thing you call the ocean."
sagesses.commentaire: We seek far off the Presence that surrounds us on every side and holds us in life at every moment. The anxious quest sometimes veils what is already there, nearer than our breath. Today, stop: that which bears you up at this very moment — have you so much as noticed it?

sagesses.texte: Two grains awaited the spring. One said: "I am afraid of the earth, of the water, of dissolving." It stayed intact at the bottom of the granary. The other let itself be buried, died, and became a field.
sagesses.commentaire: The fear of losing ourselves keeps us barren. What we hold too tightly closed within ourselves is preserved but bears no fruit. Every gift, every love, every growth asks us to accept some measure of dissolving. What do you hold back, for fear of losing yourself, that asks only to be sown?

sagesses.texte: A thief broke into a hermit's dwelling and found nothing to take. The hermit offered him his blanket, then, watching him go, sighed: "Poor man, if only I could give him this moon as well."
sagesses.commentaire: He who has tasted the riches within has nothing more to lose without, and everything to offer. True security comes not from hoarding but from being no longer the prisoner of what one owns. Today, faced with a loss or a theft, measure what remains untouched in you: that, no one can take from you.

sagesses.texte: "How does one serve the Beloved?" "Like incense," said the master: "it gives its fragrance only by burning away, and the more it spends itself, the higher its scent rises."
sagesses.commentaire: True giving has something of a burning: we spend ourselves, we seem to diminish, yet that is when we spread the best of ourselves. What is hoarded jealously perfumes nothing. Today, where can you give yourself without reckoning, knowing that it is in spending yourself that you will be most useful?

sagesses.titre: The Blind Man and the Lamp
sagesses.texte: A blind man carried a lamp at night. "What use is it to you, who cannot see?" "It is of no use to me," he said, "but to those who would otherwise cross my path without it."
sagesses.commentaire: The good we do is not always for our own lighting: sometimes it shields others without our enjoying it ourselves. The light we carry profits first those who walk near us. Today, do some deed whose benefit others will reap, with nothing drawn from it for yourself.

sagesses.texte: "My heart has run dry," complained a man. The master said: "The spring never fails; it is the stones of your worries that block its mouth. Clear them away, and the water will return."
sagesses.commentaire: Inner dryness is almost never an absence of source, but an obstruction. Anxieties, grudges, agitation form a stopper. The remedy is not to seek water elsewhere, but to remove what keeps it from welling up. What "stone" could you draw out today to let the water return?

sagesses.titre: The Two Doors of the Heart
sagesses.texte: A sheikh used to say: "The heart has two doors: one opens onto the creatures, the other onto the Real. When one opens too wide, the other closes of itself."
sagesses.commentaire: Our attention is limited: all we give to the outside, we withdraw from within, and the reverse. It is not a matter of slamming the door on the world, but of not leaving it so wide open that we forget the other. Today, which of your two doors has stayed too wide open, and at the cost of what?

sagesses.texte: "Why does the righteous one suffer?" an elder was asked. "Have you ever seen," he answered, "the goldsmith test lead in the fire? It is gold he plunges there, because he expects something of it."
sagesses.commentaire: Trial is not always chastisement; it is often the purifying of what has worth. Only precious metals are refined in the fire. This thought does not abolish pain, but gives it back a meaning. Faced with a difficulty today, ask yourself not "why me?" but "what, in me, is asking to be refined?"

sagesses.texte: A traveller complained of the dust of the road. His master said: "With no dust beneath your feet, it means you are no longer advancing. Rejoice: it is the trace of your walking."
sagesses.commentaire: The discomforts of the road bear witness that one is journeying; the motionless stay clean, but arrive nowhere. Weariness, friction, vexations are the price of movement. Today, what weighs on you in your effort may be not an obstacle to the path, but the proof that you are on it.

sagesses.texte: "How much spirituality do I need to be transformed?" The master dipped a finger into a jar of honey: "Taste. This one finger is enough to know the whole jar. Practise little, but taste truly."
sagesses.commentaire: We pile up readings, techniques, words, and let nothing soak in. Better one single practice truly savoured than a thousand skimmed over. The depth of one lived moment is worth more than the breadth of a thousand glances. What small practice, today, can you taste fully rather than stacking up ten?

sagesses.texte: The storm tore up the rigid oak and spared the reed. "See," said the master: "it is not the hardest that resists, but the most supple, the one that knows how to bow and rise again."
sagesses.commentaire: The rigidity we take for strength is often a frailty: what does not bend, breaks. True inner solidity includes suppleness, the art of yielding in time so as not to snap. Faced with a vexation today, ask yourself whether you would gain by bending a little rather than stiffening.

sagesses.texte: A sheikh kept his house always spotless. "Are you expecting someone?" "Always," he said: "and the one who sweeps without ceasing is never caught off guard by the visit."
sagesses.commentaire: To keep one's heart ready is not to put off until tomorrow the work of clarifying. He who truly awaits the Essential does not let the dust gather. Vigilance is not anxious tension, but a cleanliness kept up day after day. What small inner housekeeping can you do today, without waiting for a grand occasion?

sagesses.titre: The Two Buckets of the Well
sagesses.texte: "Sometimes I rise, sometimes I sink," lamented a disciple. The master pointed to the well: "See these two buckets: one goes down empty to come up full; without the descent, never any water drawn."
sagesses.commentaire: The phases of lowering and aridity are not the failure of the spiritual life but its rhythm. We come up laden only because we first went down, empty and low. Your hollow moments may be preparing a drawing of water. Today, if you feel "down low", do not despair: that too is where one reaches the water.

sagesses.texte: A sage walked along the seashore; the wave effaced his steps behind him. A disciple grew sad at it. "Rejoice," he said: "it is thus that one walks light, unattached to the trace one leaves."
sagesses.commentaire: Concern for the trace — reputation, merit, memory — weighs the walking down. He who acts and then lets the water efface his footprint goes forward free, unburdened of the gaze of others. Today, do a good deed and let it be effaced: recall it to no one, not even to yourself.

sagesses.texte: A water-carrier had a cracked bowl that lost half its load. Ashamed, he apologised for it. The master showed him the roadside: "See these flowers; it is your crack that watered them."
sagesses.commentaire: Our flaws, which we should like to hide, are sometimes the very channel by which a good thing spreads unknown to us. What you judge a lack in yourself may be exactly that by which you touch and nourish others. Today, look upon one of your cracks not as a shame, but as a possible passage of grace.

sagesses.texte: A king came upon a dervish sitting in the sun. "Ask me for whatever you wish." "Move out of my sun," said the dervish: "you are depriving me of what you do not possess."
sagesses.commentaire: True detachment makes one free before the powerful: whoever desires nothing of them has nothing left to fear or to beg. Voluntary poverty is a sovereignty. Today, spot a bond of dependence — a favour you hope for, an approval you watch for — and taste the freedom of doing without it.

sagesses.texte: "Where does the pearl come from?" asked the pupil. "From a grain of sand," said the master: "a wound that the oyster, unable to expel it, has patiently sheathed in nacre until it became a jewel."
sagesses.commentaire: What wounds us and cannot be cast out can, through patience, be transformed into beauty. The pearl is not the absence of pain but the pain worked, sheathed, transmuted. What splinter, today, could you cease to fight and begin, gently, to enrobe?

sagesses.titre: The Fire and the Green Wood
sagesses.texte: "Why does my heart not catch fire?" The master said: "Green wood does not take at once: first it must dry out its water. Let the tears do their work; then the flame will come."
sagesses.commentaire: Before burning with love, the heart must often purge itself of its damp attachments, its sorrows, its overflow. Apparent dryness is sometimes a necessary drying. Do not grow impatient at not "feeling": perhaps some invisible work is preparing you to catch fire. What, in you, is asking to dry out?

sagesses.texte: A disciple knew by heart all the treatises of the Way. "You are learned indeed," said the master; "but he who has memorised the map has not yet taken a single step on the road."
sagesses.commentaire: Knowledge about the path is not the path. One can hold forth on love without loving, on detachment without letting anything go. At some point one must leave the library and walk for real. What truth do you know so well in theory that you forget to practise it? Make of it, today, one real step.

sagesses.texte: "Why do I go from retreat to service, from silence to action?" The master said: "Water that sleeps grows foul; water that runs stays pure. Recollection fills you, service keeps you clear."
sagesses.commentaire: The inner life reduces neither to contemplation alone nor to action alone: it breathes between the two. Too much withdrawal and one stagnates; too much doing and one withers. The alternation keeps the current pure. Today, which of the two do you lack — silence or service — for your water to run again?

sagesses.texte: "Why am I crushed by trials?" The master said: "The whole grain nourishes no one; it must pass beneath the millstone to become bread. You are not destroyed: you are being prepared."
sagesses.commentaire: What seems to crush us sometimes works to make us useful, shareable, nourishing for others. The trial that grinds the self may be what transforms us into bread for the hungry of heart. Today, ask of your difficulty: what is it grinding in me so that I may become food?

sagesses.texte: "If I give away my light, I grow poorer," feared a man. The master lit a second candle from his own: "See: the first loses nothing, and the darkness, for its part, has drawn back twice over."
sagesses.commentaire: Spiritual good is not divided by being shared: one passes on a light without losing one's own, and the world is doubly lit. The heart's generosity is not exhausted, it is multiplied. Today, to whom could you pass on a flame — an encouragement, a knowledge, a hope — without losing any of your own?

sagesses.titre: The River That Fears the Sea
sagesses.texte: A river, come to the desert, feared losing itself in the sand. "Let the wind carry you," it was whispered: "give up your river-form, and you will fall again as rain beyond the dunes."
sagesses.commentaire: To cross certain passages, one must consent to lose the form one clings to. What seems disappearance is only a change of state: the water becomes rain again. Trust consists in letting oneself be carried through what one cannot master. What "form" of yourself must you, today, let go of to cross a desert?

sagesses.texte: Two beggars at the same door. One cried out his misery, the other kept silent, his hands open. The master of the house gave to the silent one. "Why?" "Because he was not demanding his due: he was awaiting a gift."
sagesses.commentaire: There is a way of asking that demands and a way that entrusts itself. The first closes the giver's hands, the second opens them. Before the Source as before men, the heart's bearing counts more than the words. Today, in your asking, observe: are you demanding a due, or awaiting a gift?

sagesses.texte: A leaf trembled at the thought of falling. The old tree murmured: "You do not fall into the void: you return to my roots, and next year I will make you green again, in another way."
sagesses.commentaire: The fear of the end comes from identifying with the leaf and forgetting the tree. Nothing is truly lost within the whole of which we are a part. Detachment is not indifference but trust in a vaster continuity. Faced with an ending you dread, to what greater "tree" might you know yourself bound?

sagesses.texte: "Why do my prayers stay without flame?" The master said: "You light the wick, but where is the oil? Without love to nourish the gesture, the rite is only a wick that smokes."
sagesses.commentaire: Practices without love smoke without giving light. It is not the regularity of the gestures that transforms, but the invisible oil of loving intention that feeds them. Today, before a habitual gesture — a prayer, a service, a care — take a moment to put back a little oil: for whom, for love of what, do you do it?

sagesses.titre: The Stars at the Bottom of the Well
sagesses.texte: "One sees the stars better," said the master, "from the bottom of a well than from the top of a tower." And he added: "That is why trial, which lowers us, sometimes opens the eyes that prosperity had blinded."
sagesses.commentaire: Lowering has a paradoxical virtue: it narrows the gaze and reveals what the dazzle of success had hidden. Many a heart has awakened only at the bottom of a want. If you are passing through a hollow today, ask yourself which stars, invisible from above, are beginning to appear to you there.

sagesses.texte: "Should I tear out all my weeds at once?" The master said: "No: you would uproot the good grain as well. Sow the wheat abundantly: its growth will smother the tares without your having to struggle."
sagesses.commentaire: We mend ourselves not only by fighting our faults one by one, but by cultivating the good until it takes the place. Light drives out shadow without striking it. Rather than wearing yourself out today against one failing, ask which opposing virtue you could water so that it gains the upper hand.

sagesses.texte: The gratitude of the common bears upon the benefits; that of the elect bears upon the Benefactor Himself.
sagesses.commentaire: One can give thanks for what one receives, and that is already beautiful; but higher still is to rejoice not in the gift but in the One who offers it. Love then passes from the gift to the Giver. Today, before a grace, do not stop at the pleasure received: climb back, through thankfulness, to the hand that gives.

sagesses.texte: For forty years I have stood at the door of my heart only as God's doorkeeper: none but He has entered there, none but He has gone out.
sagesses.commentaire: To keep the door of one's heart is to watch over what one lets in: thoughts, desires, presences. An inwardness inhabited by the One is not peopled with idols. Today, stand for a moment as your own doorkeeper: what did you let cross the threshold this morning, and would you have admitted it had you been on watch?

sagesses.texte: The moth that circles the flame is not content with its light nor with its heat: it finds peace only by casting itself wholly into it.
sagesses.commentaire: There are three degrees in drawing near the Source: to know its light, to feel its heat, and at last to be consumed in it. Knowledge and feeling do not suffice the one who truly loves: he wills union, even at the price of himself. Where, in your life, do you content yourself with the heat when something in you would give itself wholly?

sagesses.texte: My secret with my Beloved I have confided to no confidant: let my eyes be my witnesses, and let them keep silent.
sagesses.commentaire: The deepest experiences of the heart are not to be coined into tales; to display them too soon dispels them. There is a modesty of the intimate that preserves it. Today, what touches you most deeply, dare to keep a little for yourself, like an ember one tends rather than fanning it to the wind of words.

sagesses.titre: The Two Worlds in One Heart
sagesses.texte: O God, what draws me away from You I do not want, were it paradise itself; and what brings me near to You I do want, were it trial itself.
sagesses.commentaire: The measure of every thing is no longer pleasure or pain, but drawing near to or away from the Essential. A comfort that lulls is worth less than a hardship that awakens. Today, faced with a choice, change your criterion: ask not "what is pleasant to me?" but "what brings me nearer?"

sagesses.texte: The heart is the king of the body, and the limbs are its soldiers: set the king right, and the whole kingdom is set right.
sagesses.commentaire: We want to reform our acts without touching the source: labour lost. When the centre is true, the periphery follows; when the heart is troubled, the limbs go astray. Today, rather than correcting some surface behaviour, go down to its root: what, in your heart, truly commands it?

sagesses.texte: The veil between the servant and his Lord is neither earth nor heaven, neither the Throne nor the Footstool: it is your own self and your illusion.
sagesses.commentaire: We seek outward obstacles, circumstances, contrary destinies; the only veil is inward, the thickness of the self that takes itself for the centre. No journey crosses it: only the forgetting of self. Today, spot a place where your "self" stands as a screen, and make yourself, for a moment, transparent.
